title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2017-02-15
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL MARCH 1, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting February 15, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Doug Warren from Gulf Coast Presbyterian Church Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. RECOGNITION: Peggy Phillips, Master Naturalist, College of Life Foundation. Councilmember Errington recognized Peggy Phillips for her love, work and passion for the Estero River. Among her many accomplishments, Ms. Phillips has served as the Florida State Director of the American Canoe Association since 2015 and received the Director of the Year Award in 2016. Brandy Minchew was also recognized for her work with Ms. Phillips through the College of Life Foundation. Because of their collective involvement with the Estero River, they were responsible for the College of Life Foundation receiving the Prestigious President's Award. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 1 of9 PRESENTATION: Lee County Commissioner Larry Kiker Councilmember Boesch provided an introduction. Commissioner Kiker stated that the purchase of Edison Farms was one of the most important purchases in Lee County. He spoke regarding the property recently coming on the market; a meeting was arranged with Representative Rodriguez and Land Solutions; all County Commissioners supported efforts to explore funding options and utilizing Conservation 20/20 funds, noting that utilizing this funding limited the land to remain for preservation purposes in perpetuity. He also spoke to the property appraisals; concerns regarding possible competitors and the property being used for an extension of County Road 951; he noted that there has been no discussion regarding that or splitting the property. Commissioner Kiker added that any changes to the property would require a super majority vote. He also spoke briefly regarding maintenance of the property. Brief discussion followed. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Brown Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 3. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of February 1, 2017 Council Meeting Minutes Councilmember Wilson addressed concern regarding the roll call of the February 1, 2017 meeting indicating Councilmember Brown on an excused absence. Discussion followed regarding approval of excused absences. Village Attorney Saunders stated that the Council had not objected to the roll call at each of the meetings when Councilmember Brown was absent and it was announced as an excused absence, as well as approving the minutes. Discussion ensued. Motion: Move to correct the February 1, 2017 Council meeting minutes by reflecting that Councilmember Brown's absence as an unexcused absence. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Councilmember Ribble Action: Motion failed. Vote: Aye: Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Nay: Councilmembers Brown, Errington, Boesch, and Mayor Batos Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 2 of9 Motion: Move to approve the February 1, 2017 Council meeting minutes as presented. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the February 1, 2017 Council meeting minutes as presented. Vote: Aye: Councilmembers Brown, Errington, Boesch, and Mayor Batos Nay: Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Abstentions: Village Attorney Saunders advised that the Council may want to be a little more formal in terms of dealing with excused absence. He added that this has been an ongoing process, it has always been announced that Councilmember Brown's absence was excused, and the Council has acquiesced; from now on, since this has become an issue, the Council may want to take a formal vote. Discussion ensued regarding previous Councilmembers' excused absences and the method in which the Village Clerk announced Councilmember Brown's excused absence. It was suggested that the topic be discussed at the Council workshop next week. 4. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Resolution No. 2017-05 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Supporting Lee County's Acquisition of Edison Farms Using Lee County's Conservation 20/20 Funds and Other Available Funding Sources; Appointing Councilmember Jim Boesch as the Liaison; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-05. Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief explanation of the resolution. Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan noted that, consistent with the summary sheet and discussion at the previous Council meeting, the words "in perpetuity" needed to be added to the third "Whereas." He also requested that the resolution be revised to the extent that Councilmember Boesch not be named as the liaison; that the Council vote on the resolution be in two parts - one vote to adopt the resolution and another vote to appoint a liaison. Motion: Move that the "Whereas" clause appointing a liaison have no name in it and that the clause will indicate that the Council will appoint a liaison for the project. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Councilmember Ribble Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 3 of9 Action: The "Whereas" clause appointing a liaison will have no name in it and the clause will indicate that the Council will appoint a liaison for the project. Vote: (roll call) Aye: Councilmembers Ribble, Brown, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Nay: Councilmembers Errington, Boesch, and Mayor Batos Abstentions: Public Comment: Phil Douglas, The Brooks Meredith Barnard, Conservancy of SW Florida Joe Miceli, Rookery Pointe Don Eslick, ECCL Bill Carr, Riverwoods Plantation Michael Langella, Marsh Landing Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan and Councilmember Boesch. Discussion included the importance of the property remaining for preservation purposes in perpetuity; putting people on notice that any private group or developer would have to go through the Village first; the Village has the power to fight any development that comes forward; people have to understand that this is the beginning of a process; and this is probably one of the most important things that the Council will have to vote on. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-05 as amended: the third "Whereas" to read "Whereas, the Village Council of the Village of Estero supports Lee County in its efforts to acquire Edison Farms for preservation purposes in perpetuity" and the fourth "Whereas" to read "Whereas, the Village Council will appoint a Councilmember to act as liaison for the project." Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Vice Mayor Levitan Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-05 as amended: the third "Whereas" to read "Whereas, the Village Council of the Village of Estero supports Lee County in its efforts to acquire Edison Farms for preservation purposes in perpetuity" and the fourth "Whereas" to read "Whereas, the Village Council will appoint a Councilmember to act as liaison for the project." Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: Motion: Move to table the appointment of a liaison to another meeting. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Questions or Comments by: Councilmember Errington and Mayor Batos. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes February 15, 2017 Page 4 of9 In response to an inquiry on what would happen if a liaison was not appointed today and there was a need for one in the near future, Mayor Batos responded that the Village would have nobody as a liaison; he had understood from the previous Council meeting that there was agreement that Councilmember Boesch would be the liaison. Action: Motion failed. Vote: (roll call) Aye: Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Nay: Councilmembers Brown, Errington, Boesch, and Mayor Batos Abstentions: Motion: Move to appoint Councilmember Jim Boesch as liaison for the project. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington MayorBatos Action: Appointed Councilmember Jim Boesch as liaison for the project. Vote: (roll call) Aye: Councilmembers Brown, Errington, Boesch, and Mayor Batos Nay: Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2017-06 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Approving the Funding Agreement Between the Village of Estero and the City of Punta Gorda on Behalf of the Charlotte Harbor National Estuary Program for the Fiscal Year 2016-2017; Appointing Councilmember Katy Errington as the Liaison; Authorizing the Village Mayor to Execute the Agreement; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-05. Village Manager Sarkozy stated that the resolution was the result of a discussion at the last Council meeting. Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan addressed an amendment to Section 4 of the resolution, changing Village Manager to Mayor, and adding the word "fiscal" in the last sentence of Section 1 of the funding agreement: "The Council may extend this Agreement for two additional fiscal years ... " Village Attorney Saunders stated that the amendments were fine. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-06 as amended supporting the Village of Estero membership in the Charlotte Harbor National Estuary Program for a three year period, to be reauthorized annually, with an annual membership fee of $5,000, and appointing Councilmember Katy Errington as liaison; amending Section 4 of the resolution, changing Village Manager to Mayor, and adding the word "fiscal" in the last sentence of Section 1 of the funding agreement: "The Council may extend this Agreement for two additional fiscal years ... " Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 5 of9 Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-06 supporting the Village ofEstero membership in the Charlotte Harbor National Estuary Program for a three year period, to be reauthorized annually, with an annual membership fee of $5,000, and appointing Councilmember Katy Errington as liaison; amended Section 4 of the resolution, changed Village Manager to Mayor, and added the word "fiscal" in the last sentence of Section 1 of the funding agreement: "The Council may extend this Agreement for two additional fiscal years ... " Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: The meeting went into recess at 10:43 a.m. and reconvened at 10:50 a.m. (c) Stormwater Master Plan CN 2016-02 Consultant Agreement Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief explanation and introduced the consultant, Josh Evans, J.R. Evans Engineering. Elizabeth Fountain and Roger Kopp offered a PowerPoint presentation: existing data to be utilized; new data collection efforts; fixes of known drainage problems within Village; proposed hydrologic/hydraulic modeling approach; surface water boundaries; and groundwater boundary conditions. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Errington, Boesch, Wilson, Ribble, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included swales; the use of acronyms in the presentation; importance of the DR/GR being part of the study; the need to know whether we were contributing to the problem or helping the problem; and concerns regarding wording in the contract. Public Comment: None. Moved this agenda item to the March 1, 2017 Council meeting. ( d) Amendment to the Village of Estero Legislative Agenda Village Manager Sarkozy explained that the previously-approved agenda was communicated to the legislature; subsequently, the issue to support State funding of $3,550,000 for a Visitor and Environmental Education Center at Lovers Key State Park was brought to his attention. It was noted that this issue was brought before the Council in 2015. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 6 of9 Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to amend the recently approved Village Council Legislative Agenda to add support for State funding of$3,550,000 for a Visitor and Environmental Education Center at Lovers Key State Park. Motion by: Councilmember Boesch Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Amended the recently approved Village Council Legislative Agenda to add support for State funding of $3,550,000 for a Visitor and Environmental Education Center at Lovers Key State Park. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: Mark Generales provided comment and conveyed appreciation to the Council. Vice Mayor Levitan commented regarding boat access from his community to the park and the need for opening up to the general public. 5. ORDINANCES: (a) FIRST READING: (1) Ordinance No. 2017-02 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village ofEstero, Florida, (Approving) (Denying) Zoning and DRI Development Order Amendments for the Coconut Point Mixed Use Planned Development and Development of Regional Impact for Property Bounded by US 41 on the West, Williams Road on the North, Seminole Gulf Railroad Right-of-Way on the East and the Southern Boundary for the Village of Estero Limits, all in the Village of Estero, Florida; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2017-02. Community Development Director Gibbs explained that this was Coconut Point Tract IA; proposal for a Planned Development amendment of the Coconut Point Mixed Use Planned Development I Development of Regional Impact to allow a multifamily development of 200 dwelling units. She noted that the request was reviewed by the Planning and Zoning Board in November 2016; the Board recommended denial. In the meantime, the applicant had met with Village staff. Ms. Gibbs explained the revisions in general and spoke to three options on how Council could proceed. Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan suggested that Council continue first reading and then make a decision whether to remand to Planning and Zoning Board. Councilmember Ribble inquired what the applicant wanted to do. Neale Montgomery, Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 7 of9 Pavese Law Firm, responded that the applicant was seeking to decrease the level of impact and that it should not go back to Planning and Zoning Board. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to continue first reading of Ordinance No. 2017-02. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Councilmember Boesch Action: Continued first reading of Ordinance No. 2017-02. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: Mayor Batos commented regarding the amount of pages received in the Council meeting packet. Community Development Director Gibbs responded that staff erred on the side of caution. She also suggested that staff could reduce some of the information for first reading and provide more information at second reading. Vice Mayor Levitan said he thought the Council was not provided enough information. Brief discussion followed. 6. UNFINISHED BUSINESS: None. 7. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. 8. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble: Noted that he would be attending the upcoming Southwest Florida League of Cities meeting. He also reported that he would be meeting next week with the Village Manager and Community Development Director regarding Calvin Giordano and Associates. He also addressed concerned regarding counter personnel turnover. Vice Mayor Levitan: Addressed the discussion at the beginning of the meeting; he believed that, due to circumstances, there was a vacancy that occurred and the Charter required Council to take action within a period of time. His hope was that Council could discuss this fully and completely at a future point and address whether Mr. Brown was capable of doing the job that he was elected to do. Councilmember Errington: Stated that she believed that everyone needed to look within and ask whether what Council was asking was morally right. She disagreed with what she experienced at today's meeting. She also believed that this should have been addressed months ago. Councilmember Boesch: Stated that he was stunned by the method the liaison issue was handled today. He noted that very successful meetings regarding Edison Farms have been held since the last Council meeting. He also commented regarding a traffic backup on Three Oaks Parkway that occurred yesterday. He suggested that Mayor Batos write a Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 15, 2017 Page 8 of9 letter regarding Edison Farms as other local Mayors have done. Mayor Batos responded that, due to today's motion on Resolution No. 2017-05, he would write a letter. Councilmember Wilson: Conveyed congratulations to the Design Review Board for a landscape plan that they worked on with a developer. Mayor Batos: Referred to information regarding a future Metropolitan Planning Organization meeting and noted that if anyone had anything to add to let him know. He spoke regarding Councilmember Brown, noting his service to the community. He also said that he has attended every meeting that he could and he was looking forward to continuing to serve the community. He believed that Councilmember Brown's goal to come back to Council had given him the strength to continue his fight against cancer. In conclusion, he asked Council to treat Mr. Brown with compassion and brotherhood and let him decide when he can no longer serve the community. 9. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. 10. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: Introduced Bob Franceschini, who will be working on Village contracts on a part time basis. (a) Lee Health - Coconut Point Medical Center Truck Route and Management Plan Village Manager Sarkozy provided information regarding the sand trucks route to minimize impacts due to the Lee Health Coconut Point project construction. Vice Mayor Levitan addressed concern regarding the traffic light at Corkscrew Road and US 41 and the impacts of vehicles turning left from the Publix shopping center and inquired regarding asking the county to suspend the left out. Mayor Batos spoke to a concern regarding the round-about on Williams Road in the morning and afternoon. Brief discussion followed. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 11. title: Joint Workshop Minutes - 2017-02-28
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- flgov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL MARCH 15, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero and City of Bonita Springs Joint Workshop Estero Fire Rescue 21500 Three Oaks Parkway Estero, FL 33928 February 28, 2017 10:00 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER AND WELCOME: 10:00 a.m. - Mayors Batos and Simmons 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Mayor Simmons. 3. ROLL CALL: Present - Village ofEstero: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5 (excused absence). Present - City of Bonita Springs: Mayor Peter Simmons, Deputy Mayor Peter O'Flinn - District 4, Council Member Amy Quaremba - District 1, Council Member Greg DeWitt - District 2, Council Member Steven Slachta - District 3, Council Member Michael Gibson - District 5, and Council Member Fred Forbes, AIA - District 6. Also present - Village ofEstero: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Assistant Village Attorney Derek Rooney, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. City of Bonita Springs: City Manager Carl Schwing and City Attorney Audrey Vance. 4. INTRODUCTION OF COUNCIL MEMBERS 5. PUBLIC COMMENT: None. Joint Workshop: Village Council and Bonita Springs City Council Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 28, 2017 Page 1 of 4 6. BACKGROUND AND HOW WE GOT HERE: Introductory remarks were made by Mayor Batos and Deputy Mayor O'Flinn. In late November, 2016, a short presentation regarding SUNTrail was given at a joint meeting of the Lee and Charlotte Counties Metropolitan Planning Organizations. Both Mayor Batos and Deputy Mayor O'Flinn believed that the SUNTrail was an important initiative, with the potential to become a first class, multimodal-use path connecting Estero and Bonita Springs; the joint workshop would provide an opportunity for both Councils and the public to have the benefit of information and develop a sense of whether to continue to explore the possibilities. It was explained that SUNTrail is a plan for a statewide system of safe, connected multi-use paths. Funding for the program began in 2015; the City of Cape Coral was awarded funds for the portion of SUNTrail in their area. It was noted that the imprint of the SUNTrail in the Estero-Bonita Springs area is on Seminole Gulf Railway tracks, with focus from Estero Parkway to Bonita Beach Road; however, there were other alternative connections that could be made available. It was also noted that Seminole Gulf Railway representatives were amenable to talking about full acquisition, easement, etc., and that CSX was open to conversation as well. 7. PRESENTATION ON FLORIDA SUNTRAIL SYSTEM-DALE ALLEN, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, FLORIDA GREENWAYS AND TRAILS FOUNDATION: Mr. Allen provided background information regarding the Florida Greenways and Trails Foundation, including corporate sponsors and foundation funders and sponsors. The foundation is Florida's chief advocacy organization for a statewide system of non- motorized trails and greenways. The foundation wants to tum Florida into the world's #1 greenway and trail destination. The foundation supports trails for paddling, hiking, equestrian, dirt and mountain bike, and Shared Use Non-motorized (SUNTrails) paved regional connectors, as well as expanding public access for all Florida residents and visitors, especially the Gold Medal state parks, public lands, and conservation areas. Mr. Allen explained that "greenways" means the land corridors that allow safe, non- motorized passage of people and wildlife; "trails" means the actual footpath or paved surface through the greenway. Mr. Allen stated that 200 miles of the proposed 250 miles Coast-to-Coast Connector (C2C) are already in place; work was currently underway to close all of the seven gaps on the C2C; and economic impact of trails on rural and mid-sized communities will be dramatic. He spoke to the tourism, economic, and public health benefits gained through connecting this emerging priority trail system to Florida's Gold Medal Award winning state parks and unique conservation lands. He also spoke to the miles of active railroad lines in the United States at the tum of the 20th century which began to unravel beginning with the Great Depression and accelerating after WWII. He addressed the Florida abandoned railroad rights-of-way, noting that may of the corridors have been converted to rail-trails. He pointed out Florida trails along waterfronts and connected trails across North America and around the world and addressed basic and expanded economics of paved trails, as well as the "Three S's Test": a successful trail is safe, scenic, and Sun- sational. Examples of Sun-sational places, bridges, downtowns, and events were provided. Joint Workshop: Village Council and Bonita Springs City Council Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 28, 2017 Page 2 of4 Regarding existing trail funding opportunities, Mr. Allen spoke to the strong support that exists in Florida Legislature for regionally connected trails, pursuant to Section 335.065(4)F; SB 2514-A; and Florida Department of Transportation approval of 45 projects on the SUNTrails priority system and allocation of $44.5 million to begin work on trail elements. He also spoke regarding the Coast to Coast Regional Connector; provided maps of the statewide system that is already in place; indicated that there are 25 major gaps; and spoke to lessons from C2C Trail and observations regarding SWC2. In conclusion, the following points were made: regional paved trails are happening in Florida; SUNTrails Program is transformative legislation and must be protected; local governments must organize regionally; additional public funding is needed, especially for side and connector trails at the community level; funding is also needed for trail necessities; funding for management of trails is also problematic. He also addressed funding: Constitutional Amendment One passed overwhelmingly on November 4, 2014 by Florida voters; Ten Percent for Trails - ask legislators to allocate funding from 2014 Constitutional Amendment One; recommendations from the Florida Greenways and Trails Foundation on how the 10% should be committed were provided, as well as economic, tourism, and safety goals of paved trails for Florida. 8. COUNCIL QUESTIONS: Discussion followed regarding safety record to date; experience with local cities; average cost for one mile of trail; how to make intersections safe; minimum building standards and safety features; steps to get going; maintenance and replacement costs; availability of grant funds; asphalt vs. concrete; purchasing railroad right-of-way; references for all of the great ideas in archives, rails with trails, and engineering resources; and shading of trails. 9. PRESENTATION ON SUNTRAIL FROM LEE COUNTY MPO PERSPECTIVE - DON SCOTT, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, LEE COUNTY METROPOLITAN PLANNING ORGANIZATION: Mr. Scott addressed and provided information regarding the following maps: Lee County Corridors from 2015 Office of Green ways Land Trail Opportunity; Southwest Coastal Regional Trail; Land Trail Opportunity; Priority Trail; Area Attractions map; The Coastal Loop - addition to the Southwest Coastal Regional Trail; Trail along Seminole Gulf Railroad Right-of-Way- in Estero and Bonita Springs, the SW Coastal Regional Trail is proposed along the Seminole Gulf Railroad right-of-way from Alico Road to Riverside Park; and Lee County MPO Rail Feasibility Study map. Mr. Scott spoke to the development of the SUNTrail Network/Southwest Coast Connector Priorities; SUNTrail Network Timeline; SUNTrail Project Applications; the Kismet Parkway Multi-Use Trail Project; and the John Yarbrough Trail Extension. 10. COUNCIL QUESTIONS: Discussion followed regarding timeline and the possibility of updating the maps in the near future; materials preference; resurfacing maintenance; there were no matching Joint Workshop: Village Council and Bonita Springs City Council Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 28, 2017 Page 3 of4 funds; MPO railroad right-of-way study; and the interest shown by the Seminole Gulf Railway representatives. 11. PUBLIC COMMENT: Richard Shine, Happehatchee Center, questions regarding future integration and methodology for funds; safety and mobility. Doug Saxton, The Reserve, spoke to the importance of the rail to trail environment for safety and inquired how he could others to get this moved forward. Tom Barber, Estero resident, conveyed appreciation of this process. Dallas Revord, FineMark National Bank and Trust, inquired regarding railway crossings and what the railway's incentive would be. Jim Royer spoke to his reasons for residing in a small town. Dena Wilcoxen, Realtor, stated that an overwhelming question has been the location of the bike path and walk path. She also believed that the paths increased property value. 12. COUNCIL COMMENTS AND TAKE-A WAYS: Mayor Batos expressed pleasure in participating in the first joint meeting between the two Councils and hoped that this was something that would continue as the years go by. He believed that Estero and Bonita Springs were beautiful and strong communities; together they can be even stronger. Mayor Simmons echoed Mayor Batos' sentiments, indicated that one of the highest priorities of the new Bonita Springs Council was meeting with the Village of Estero, and hoped that it was the first of many joint meetings. He agreed that Bonita Springs and Estero were fantastic communities, together were stronger and could offer more for the public and the citizens. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 13. ADJOURNMENT: 11:44 a.m. ATTEST: By: , ~Ll).__, KathyH~ C, Village Clerk VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA Nicholas Batos, Mayor Joint Workshop: Village Council and Bonita Springs City Council Final Action Agenda/Minutes - February 28, 2017 Page 4 of4

raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. APPROVED BY COUNCIL APRIL 5, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting March 1, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Village Manager Sarkozy provided information received via Councilmember Brown's wife indicating that the doctor appointment he had scheduled this afternoon had been moved up; as a result he had to go to Tampa earlier than expected and requested an excused absence. Village Attorney Saunders stated that it was up to the Council to determine whether the absence would be excused. Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmernber Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmernber Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown, District 3, excused absence. At the conclusion of the roll call, Mayor Batos inquired whether any Councilmember objected to Councilmember Brown's excused absence; no objections were noted. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- March 1, 2017 Page 1 of5 Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Positive comments in favor of Councilmember Brown: Justin Zinn, Terra Vista Greg Toth, no address provided Beverly MacNellis, Marsh Landing Jean McLoughlin, Grand Bayou Court Dick Schweers, Fountain Lakes, Estero Fire Rescue Board Commissioner District 3 representation concerns: Ben Korbly, The Colony Barry Freedman, The Vines Gloria Beck, The Vines Vicki Olson, The Colony Norm Berger, The Vines; also spoke regarding concerns related to use of the railroad right-of-way for a bike and walkway Mayor Batos conveyed appreciation to everyone that offered comments and noted that Councilmembers served all districts in the Village; if anybody had a problem, they could contact any of the Councilmembers. Councilmember Errington added that she assisted residents in District 3 on two occasions after receiving one email and one phone call and believed it was up to the Council to support Councilmember Brown. 4. INTERVIEWS: Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board Applicants Prior to conducting interviews, Council discussed observations of recent conflicts of interest and performance issues regarding two Board Members; and the Council's hearing process related to removal of Board Members. The following applicants were interviewed: Barry Jones Patty Whitehead Bill Prysi Anthony Gargano Scott Anderson Dell Jones John Yarbrough Applicant Mark Novitski was not available. Questions of applicants included attendance, conflicts of interest, advocacy, and treatment of the public. The meeting went into recess at 11:12 and reconvened at 11:20 a.m. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- March 1, 2017 Page 2 of5 Discussion ensued related to whether to continue discussion until the next Council meeting or vote to fill the current vacancies. Motion: Move to schedule a hearing to speak to two Board Members, one on the Planning and Zoning Board and one on the Design Review Board, regarding whether they should be removed. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Councilmember Boesch Questions or Comments by: Mayor Batos, Vice Mayor Levitan, Councilmembers Wilson, Errington, and Boesch. Discussion included the type of notice to be provided; Village Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud's role; and the hearing process, which would entail a discussion with the two Board Members about their roles, behaviors, and serving at the pleasure of the Council. It was noted that both Board Members have faithfully served the Village for a number of years. Action: A hearing will be scheduled to speak to two Board Members, one on the Planning and Zoning Board and one on the Design Review Board, regarding whether they should be removed. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Through a ballot process, Anthony Gargano and John Yarbrough were appointed to the Planning and Zoning Board, terms ending March 31, 2020; Scott Anderson, Barry Jones, Bill Prysi, and Patty Whitehead were appointed to the Design Review Board, terms ending March 31, 2019. 5. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of February 15, 2017 Council Meeting Minutes (b) Financial Report for January 31, 2017 Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- March l, 2017 Page 3 of5 Questions or Comments by: Councilmember Wilson and Vice Mayor Levitan. 6. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Stormwater Master Plan CN 2016-02 Consultant Agreement Village Manager Sarkozy reviewed the information from the last Council meeting and noted that the suggestions for revisions provided by the Council were incorporated into the agreement. Motion: Move to approve the agreement with J .R. Evans Engineering for preparation of a Stormwater Master Plan for the Village, to be completed by approximately July 2018. Motion by: Councilmember Errington Seconded by: Councilmember Ribble Action: Approved the agreement with J .R. Evans Engineering for preparation of a Stormwater Master Plan for the Village, to be completed by approximately July 2018. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 7. UNFINISHED BUSINESS: None. 8. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Mike St John, Stoneybrook, conveyed disappointment with the way Councilmember Brown was treated at the last Council meeting. He also addressed consideration of election of a Mayor at large. Village Manager Sarkozy introduced Patty Narozny from the Estero Fine Art Show. Ms. Narozny addressed the Council regarding approval to allow street signage on a permit basis for an upcoming art show at Miromar Design Center, which she described as a top economic event in Estero. Brief discussion followed. A workshop will be scheduled at an upcoming Council meeting. 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble: spoke regarding the new FGCU President, Michael Martin. Councilmember Errington: requested a workshop for the Estero River Safety Committee in order to provide information and present a request to Council. Mayor Batos: spoke regarding the February 28 workshop and believed that it was well- received. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- March 1, 2017 Page 4 of5 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Village Attorney Saunders addressed Senate Bills and House Bills in the upcoming legislative session. He also noted that when he is in Tallahassee, Derek Rooney will be covering Council meetings for him. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 12. ADJOURNMENT: 12:05 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: ~J..JALL_, Kathy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- March 1, 2017 Page 5 of5

title: Village Council Workshop Minutes - 2016-03-02
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village "Clerk. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero-fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL MARCH 16, 2016 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 21500 Three Oaks Parkway Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Workshop March 2, 2016 10:00 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER: 10:00 a.m. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Mayor Batos. 2. INVOCATION: Offered by Pastor Nolen Rollins from Legacy Church. 3. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Village Finance Director Lisa Pace, and Village Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 4. APPROVAL OF AGENDA: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 2, 2016 Page 1 of4 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS: (A) Introduction to Budget and CIP Process Village Manager Sarkozy introduced the topic and stated that this was an important kickoff to the Budget and CIP Process; he urged caution in the first year, suggested that Council take a conservative approach, and said there was no intent to commit the Council to anything at this point in time. Village Finance Director Pace provided information related to Budget Calendar; Preliminary Fund Balance projections for October 1, 2016 for General Fund and Capital Project Fund; Projected Amounts for Capital Projects as of October 1, 2016; Potential Accumulation of Reserves in Capital Improvement Plan; and Gas Tax for Road Maintenance. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Ribble, Boesch, Wilson, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included Estero official population - seasonal versus non- seasonal; various County services; and Estero roads. Mayor Batos noted that Lee County Commissioners requested each municipality to submit a list of capital requirements by April 1. He said that, since the Village did not currently have a priority list and, in order to fulfill the request from the County, he suggested that Council take at least from the study received as being the priority for now, the following: Estero Parkway sidewalk situation, Estero Parkway road situation, the other two roads that were identified as being in the worst condition, and possible temporary fixes on Corkscrew Road, not in any particular order. Discussion ensued and there was consensus to proceed with the priority list as stated. Village Manager Sarkozy stated that a letter would be prepared for the Mayor's signature and he intended to follow up with County staff and begin to lay out a program that addressed the concerns mentioned. Discussion continued. Public Comment: None. (B) Review ofEstero Infrastructure Inventory Prepared by FGCU Village Manager Sarkozy referred to the map entitled "Pavement by Crack Type" and noted that there were relatively few roads with severe cracking. He noted that Lee County's ranking coincided with FGCU's ranking. He stated that one possible response to the study was to collaborate with the County in some way to figure out how to resurface those streets. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Errington, Ribble, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan. Discussion included gateway signs, landscaping and dripline irrigation; road safety and maintenance; lobbying other entities to do their part as well; and the property at Broadway A venue and Trailside Drive. Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 2, 2016 Page 2 of4 Mayor Batos asked that Councilmembers provide their lists to the Village Manager. Public Comment: None. (C) Village Center Planning Process Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction. He stated that the planning process was anticipated to be about a year in length and the intent was to provide the Council a timeline and introduce the four major elements of the process. Development Director Gibbs spoke to: meetings held with developers and the public meetings; higher density; commercially zoned property; tall structures; responses to recent survey; Council's vision of high quality and gathering spaces; consultant's recommendation for basic road network connections; incentive offer concept; and an explanation of the comprehensive plan process and land development code process. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Ribble and Errington. Discussion included public notice and the Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board joint workshop on March 9. The workshop went into recess at 11:33 a.m. and reconvened at 11:39 a.m. Public Comment: Steve Hartsell, representing Focus Development Gerard Ripo, representing the Downtown Estero project Jim Wallace, Genova Neale Montgomery, Pavese Law Firm Greg Toth, Estero property investor Andrew D'Jamoos, representing The Village Partners (D) Council Rules of Procedure Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction and explanation. Attorney Derek Rooney noted that he had received some recommended changes. Questions or Comments by: Mayor Batos, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, and Boesch. Discussion included packet materials deadline 7 days in advance of meeting date; materials in electronic format; meeting days; speaker time limits; and special meetings. Public Comment: None. Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 2, 2016 Page 3 of 4 6. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Appearance by: Beverly MacNellis, Marsh Landing resident: Grand opening of the 1904 Schoolhouse, Saturday, March 5 7. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble provided an update regarding the move to the new Village Hall. Brief discussion followed regarding emergency procedures. Vice Mayor Levitan spoke to the Planning and Zoning Board vacancy and noted that some other Board Member terms would be expiring on March 31, 2016. Brief discussion followed regarding attendance of Board Members. Councilmember Errington complimented the Village Manager on the hurricane disaster preparation plan that they discussed; spoke to the fracking issue and commended the Council and all who came forward to help and took a strong stand; reported regarding Estero River cleaning; and addressed frustration regarding the Village Center. Councilmember Wilson echoed Councilmember Errington's comments regarding the fracking issue; conveyed appreciation to the Village Manager and Finance Director for the financial information; and encouraged the Mayor regarding the roads issue. Mayor Batos read a letter that was received from a young resident regarding the need for a nearby hospital. He also shared information regarding a meeting he attended related to Lake Okeechobee releases and suggested that Phil Flood from South Florida Water Management District be invited to an upcoming Council meeting. 8. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS: None. 9. VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS: None. 10. It applies to members of advisory and non-advisory bodies who are presented with a voting conflict of interest under Section 112.3143, Florida Statutes. Your responsibilities under the law when faced with voting on a measure in which you have a conflict of interest will vary greatly depending on whether you hold an elective or appointive position. For this reason, please pay close attention to the instructions on this form before completing and filing the form. INSTRUCTIONS FOR COMPLIANCE WITH SECTION 112.3143, FLORIDA STATUTES A person holding elective or appointive county, municipal, or other local public office MUST ABSTAIN from voting on a measure which would inure to his or her special private gain or loss. Each elected or appointed local officer also MUST ABSTAIN from knowingly voting on a measure which would inure to the special gain or loss of a principal (other than a government agency) by whom he or she is retained (including the parent, subsidiary, or sibling organization of a principal by which he or she is retained); to the special private gain or loss of a relative; or to the special private gain or loss of a business associate. Commissioners of community redevelopment agencies (CRAs) under Sec. 163.356 or 163.357, F.S., and officers of independent special tax districts elected on a one-acre, one-vote basis are not prohibited from voting in that capacity. For purposes of this law, a "relative" includes only the officer's father, mother, son, daughter, husband, wife, brother, sister, father-in-law, mother-in-law, son-in-law, and daughter-in-law. A "business associate" means any person or entity engaged in or carrying on a business enterprise with the officer as a partner, joint venturer, coowner of property, or corporate shareholder (where the shares of the corporation are not listed on any national or regional stock exchange). **************** ELECTED OFFICERS: In addition to abstaining from voting in the situations described above, you must disclose the conflict: PRIOR TO THE VOTE BEING TAKEN by publicly stating to the assembly the nature of your interest in the measure on which you are abstaining from voting; and WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER THE VOTE OCCURS by completing and filing this form with the person responsible for recording the minutes of the meeting, who should incorporate the form in the minutes. **************** APPOINTED OFFICERS: Although you must abstain from voting in the situations described above, you are not prohibited by Section 112.3143 from otherwise participating in these matters. However, you must disclose the nature of the conflict before making any attempt to influence the decision, whether orally or in writing and whether made by you or at your direction. IF YOU INTEND TO MAKE ANY ATTEMPT TO INFLUENCE THE DECISION PRIOR TO THE MEETING AT WHICH THE VOTE WILL BE TAKEN: • You must complete and file this form (before making any attempt to influence the decision) with the person responsible for recording the minutes of the meeting, who will incorporate the form in the minutes. (Continued on page 2) CE FORM 8B-EFF. 11/2013 Adopted by reference in Rule 34-7.010(1 )(f), F.A.C. PAGE 1 APPOINTED OFFICERS (continued) • A copy of the form must be provided immediately to the other members of the agency. • The form must be read publicly at the next meeting after the form is filed. IF YOU MAKE NO ATTEMPT TO INFLUENCE THE DECISION EXCEPT BY DISCUSSION AT THE MEETING: • You must disclose orally the nature of your conflict in the measure before participating. • You must complete the form and file it within 15 days after the vote occurs with the person responsible for recording the minutes of the meeting, who must incorporate the form in the minutes. A copy of the form must be provided immediately to the other members of the agency, and the form must be read publicly at the next meeting after the form is filed. title: Village Council Workshop Minutes - 2017-03-09
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL APRIL 5, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Workshop March 9, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. 0 PENIN G EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 (via teleconference; left the meeting at 12:23 p.m.), Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 9, 2017 Page 1 of3 4. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Charles Dauray, Estero Historic Preservation Citizens Committee, provided a report of the summary of the work and direction that the committee has gone since July 2015. Mr. Dauray also spoke to the committee's shift in emphasis in identifying historic structures. 3. WORKSHOP ITEM: • Council Rules of Procedure, Liaison Roles, and Related Organizational Issues Section 3: Revisions from the February 22, 2017 workshop were reviewed. Discussion ensued regarding Councilmembers participating in meetings and quasi-judicial hearings by telephone. Additional revisions were made to 3 (h) relating to participating by telephone. Sections 6 and 7: Revisions from the February 22, 2017 workshop were reviewed; further revisions were made to 6 (c) related to agenda supporting documentation, 6 (f) related to placement of subject matter on an agenda, and 7 (b) and ( e) related to special meetings and emergency meetings. Village Land Use Attorney Stroud and Community Development Director Gibbs reviewed suggested revisions to Section 10. Discussion included adding language for speakers' time limits at hearings; revising the last sentence of 10 (g); and adding the words "Land Use" in the first sentence of 10 (1): " ... and any recommendations of the Village staff and appropriate Village Land Use Boards." The meeting went into recess at 11:06 a.m. and reconvened at 11:14 a.m. Review of Sections 11 through 17 followed. Discussion included proposed revisions to Section 13 related to role of the MayorNice Mayor, particularly serving as the spokesperson for the Village. In response to an inquiry, Village Attorney Saunders responded that selecting a spokesperson for the Village was a matter of policy and did not violate provisions of the charter. The Village Attorney and Village Manager provided suggestions on rewording 12 (e); at the beginning of the paragraph add "Except where a liaison is appointed, or the Council appoints a spokesperson on an issue ... " Mr. Saunders explained that it would leave the Mayor as a spokesperson only where there has not been an appointment of a liaison and would serve as spokesperson only on resolutions, ordinances and policies that have been adopted. Village Manager Sarkozy believed that the wording emphasized the role of liaison and recognized that on some routine issues the Mayor may be questioned and would be able to respond what was done by the Council, as well as keeping the intent outlined in Section 12. Further discussion related to Section 13 involved deleting the words "or the public" from the first sentence of 13 (e) paragraph two and adding the word "social" to the last sentence of 13 (e) paragraph two: " ... attendance at non-profit organization social functions, such as the Estero Chamber of Commerce, and other similar social or business sponsored events ... " Section 14: Discussion included relaxed guidelines for Councilmember discussion during workshops and the possibility of expanding the guidelines for public comment. Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes-March 9, 2017 Page 2 of3 Section 17: Discussion included liaisons for on-going issues; the May 1 annual appointment schedule and length of appointment; liaison selection process; and sunshine law as it relates to the liaison role. Discussion to be continued at the next workshop. 4. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. 5. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Errington: Reported that she did not receive notice regarding today's Charlotte Harbor National Estuary Program and, therefore, would not be attending; she will also not be able to attend the May 11, 2017 meeting and Mayor Batos will be attending on her behalf. Mayor Batos: Reported that he will be out of town next Friday and Councilmember Boesch will be attending the MPO meeting on his behalf. Spoke regarding the February 28, 2017 joint workshop with Bonita Springs Council and the request that each Council bring up at a meeting whether to continue with the research on the findings and to appoint a liaison. He stated that he would be asking to be appointed as the liaison and asked that the Village Manager place this on the next Council meeting agenda. He also stated that either he or Councilman O'Flinn would be required to resign from the MPO and that Councilman O'Flinn said that he would be stepping down. 6. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. 7. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 8. ADJOURNMENT: 12:47 p.m. ATTEST: By: ~UN KathyHaii,c, Village Clerk VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA Village Council Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes -March 9, 2017 Page3 of3

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2017-03-15
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL APRIL 19, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting March 15, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos Invocation: Pastor Sheri Weston from River of Life Assembly of God Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown, District 3, excused absence. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda, with the removal of Agenda Item 11 ( e ). Motion by: Councilmember Boesch Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the agenda as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 1 of7 Village Manager Sarkozy spoke to the support of the Estero Fire Rescue during the recent Collier County fire. Brief discussion followed. 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Amber Crooks, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, spoke regarding bills that the conservancy is tracking through the legislative session related to the ban on well stimulation treatments for oil and gas. She also requested Council consideration of a letter to Senator Passidorno and Representative Rodrigues to be written and sent along with copies of the Council resolution and ordinance related to fracking. Mayor Batos referenced the letter that was distributed to Council. Vice Mayor Levitan stated that he wanted time to review the letter, since it had just been distributed to Council at the meeting. Discussion ensued regarding Senator Passidorno's bill related to Horne Rule. Mayor Batos requested that, if any of the Councilrnernbers had a problem with the letter, to pass along their concerns to the Village Manager; he would wait a few days before mailing the letter. 4. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of February 22, 2017 Council Workshop Minutes (b) Approval of February 28, 2017 Joint Workshop with Bonita Springs Council Minutes Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilrnernber Errington Councilrnernber Boesch Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilrnernber Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 5. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Appointment of Liaison to SUN Trail Project Village Manager Sarkozy explained that this was a follow up to the February 28, 2017 joint workshop with Bonita Springs City Council; motions would be needed to support the efforts to secure the SUN Trail for a multi-purpose trail and to appoint a liaison. Public Comment: Norm Berger, President, The Vines Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 2 of7 Motion: Move to support the project and continue with fact finding. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved support for the project and to continue fact finding. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Mayor Batos reviewed that, at the February 28, 2017 workshop, it was asked that he and Peter O'Flinn, Bonita Springs Councilmember, continue as liaisons. Motion: Move to appoint Mayor Batos as liaison. Motion by: Councilmember Boesch Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Ribble, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included whether Peter O'Flinn or Mayor Batos would be stepping down from the Metropolitan Planning Organization; the importance of knowing all of the ramifications prior to appointing the liaison; and the revised Council meeting agenda that was distributed on March 13, 201 7. Substitute Motion: Move to table appointment of a liaison. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Councilmember Ribble Action: Appointment of a liaison was tabled. Vote: (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Aye: Councilmembers Ribble, Boesch, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan Nay: Councilmember Errington and Mayor Batos Abstentions: Brief discussion ensued regarding appointment of liaisons. 6. UNFINISHED BUSINESS: None. 7. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. 8. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble: Spoke regarding the FGCU foundation endowment. Councilmember Errington: Spoke regarding the March 21, 2017 Planning and Zoning Board meeting regarding the comprehensive plan. She noted that the Estero Historic Preservation Citizens Committee has been looking at the historic area, which she believed was critical to designate. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 3 of7 Councilmember Boesch: Addressed concern regarding Senator Passidomo's bill related to Home Rule that was moving forward. Discussion followed related to the numerous emails Council has received; concerns being heard through the Florida League of Cities contacts; and the Village Attorney's upcoming meeting with key legislators to discuss impacts. 9. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Village Attorney Saunders reported on the legislative session in Tallahassee. He also indicated that he would be providing a report to Council next week. 10. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: (a) 2016 Staff Projects Annual Report Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief overview of the report that included major accomplishments and highlights from 2016 as well as a preview for 2017. He indicated that the report will also be available on the Village website. 11. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Estero River Maintenance - Roland Ottolini, Director, Lee County Natural Resources Division Roland Ottolini offered a PowerPoint Presentation and spoke to Lee County objectives for creek maintenance; 2016 and 2017 scope of work; and 2017 proposal and scope of work. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Errington and Boesch. Discussion included a description of the north and south branch; river clean up performed by the Estero River Boating Committee at the railroad bridge; and possible maintenance further upstream. (b) Estero Parkway-Danny Nelson, Tetra Tech Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction and explained that Tetra Tech was chosen from the pool of engineers that Council previously approved. Danny Nelson offered a PowerPoint Presentation and spoke to information regarding the firm and team; Estero Parkway capital component of interlocal agreement background information; Estero Parkway existing conditions and needed improvements; phased approach - Phase 1 - road and sidewalk/linear park and Phase 2 - beautification; conceptual design; evaluation phase; design and implementation - Package 1 - roadway and sidewalk improvement and Package 2 - roadway beautification; and considerations by Council, including standards for landscaping, lighting and road signs; workshops and stakeholder meetings; and adoption of standards and guidelines. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 4 of7 Questions or Comment: Councilmembers Ribble, Boesch Wilson Errington ' ' ' Vice Mayor Levitan, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included widespread communication regarding the project; project funding; landscaping; the project being unique and an example for future projects; and lighting. Public Comment: Darla Letourneau, BikeWalkLee Doug Saxton, The Reserve, BikeWalkLee Patty Whitehead, Old Estero Marian Rosencrans, Breckenridge Marie Catanese, The Cascades Don Eslick, ECCL Bill Carr, Riverwoods Plantation Joe Miceli, Rookery Pointe Brice Ehrnig, FGCU Gary Green, Pensacola A venue Questions or Comments: Vice Mayor Levitan and Councilmembers Boesch and Errington. Discussion included the firm that the Village selected for the project; narrowing roads; and Estero Parkway improvement will be a long range plan. The meeting went into recess at 11:31 a.m. and reconvened at 11:42 a.m. (c) Fee Reductions as an Incentive in Village Center Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs reviewed the Village Center land use category as it relates to incentives and spoke to the idea of partnering with developers of projects on a case by case basis to incentivize the types of development the Village desires; one type of suggested incentive has been a partial reduction of building permit fees. Ms. Gibbs noted that permit fees, listed in the fee schedule adopted by resolution, did not provide for any reduction or waivers; if a reduction was desired, Council would need to amend the fee schedule. Community Development Director Gibbs continued that a proposal from Mr. Wallace, Genova Partners, LLC, was requesting a waiver of fees for the parking that would be underground and a fee reduction for hiring a private provider company to conduct certain building inspections and plan reviews. Ms. Gibbs added that Florida statutes do not address this and local jurisdictions do not allow for fee reductions. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Wilson, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included setting precedent; incentives; benefit of underground parking; subsidizing or raising fees; possibility of a grant fund out of the general fund; developers' choice to hire a private provider to perform inspections; guest parking; staffing needs for building inspections; whether Lee Health would be requesting a fee waiver; length of time Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 5 of7 for building completion; no other municipality or the county was allowing a waiver or fee reduction; and impact fees. Public Comment: Jim Wallace, Genova (d) Developments in the DR/GR Area Village Manager Sarkozy explained that this workshop item was intended as a general discussion. Community Development Director Gibbs reviewed the history of the DR/GR (Density Reduction/Groundwater Resource), a description of where it lies, and provided a summary of the projects ( as provided in the staff report). Questions or Comments: Vice Mayor Levitan, Councilmembers Boesch, Wilson, Errington, Ribble, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included water flow to Estero; negotiating an interlocal agreement with the county; increased vehicle trips; local schools at capacity; affecting the quality oflife for Estero residents; and clear consensus of the Council that this is of great concern and something needs to be done. Community Development Director noted that the Scope of Services for County Traffic Study was included in the staff report which includes an analysis of Corkscrew Road and developing a proportionate share for some of the developments, and environmental enhancement including restoration of flowway. Public Comment: Sean McCabe, Conservancy of Southwest Florida Patty Whitehead, Old Estero Don Eslick, ECCL Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe Peter Cangialosi, The Preserve Bill Carr, Riverwoods Plantation Brief discussion followed on how to proceed. Village Manager Sarkozy spoke to the stormwater study that would provide useful data to understand stormwater flow, groundwater infiltration, as well as saltwater intrusion. He also spoke to other issues that the county would need to address. He indicated that staff would provide further detailed analysis. (e) Review of Conflicts oflnterest and Performance This workshop item was removed from the agenda. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 6 of7 12. ADJOURNMENT: 1:15 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: .1~~ Kathy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 15, 2017 Page 7 of7

APPROVED BY COUNCIL APRIL 6, 2016 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 21500 Three Oaks Parkway Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Regular Meeting March 16, 2016 10:00 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER: 10:00 a.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Mayor Batos. 3. INVOCATION: Offered by Pastor Doug Warren from Gulf Coast Presbyterian Church. 4. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 (excused absence). Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 5. APPROVAL OF AGENDA: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Regular Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 16, 2016 Page 1 of6 6. CONSENT AGENDA: (A) Approval of Minutes (1) February 10, 2016 Regular Meeting (2) February 24, 2016 Regular Meeting (3) February 24, 2016 Workshop (4) March 2, 2016 Workshop Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 7. COUNCIL BUSINESS: (A) Phil Flood, Intergovernmental and Community Outreach Representative-Principal, South Florida Water Management District Due to difficulties with the meeting room equipment, the presentation will be rescheduled. (B) Comprehensive Annual Financial Report for the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2015 Village Manager Sarkozy introduced Martin Redovan of CliftonLarsonAllen LLP. Mr. Redovan provided an explanation of the independent auditors' report on internal control over financial reporting and on compliance and other matters based on an audit of financial statements performed in accordance with Government Auditing Standards. Discussion included the scope of the testing on internal control and compliance, and the result of the testing. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Wilson and Boesch. Public Comment: None. Village Council Regular Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 16, 2016 Page 2 of6 Motion: Move to accept the report. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Councilmember Ribble Action: Accepted the report. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (C) Resolution No. 2016-06 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village ofEstero, Florida, Accepting the Hertz Corporate Center Replat; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-06 and Community Development Director Gibbs provided a brief explanation. Questions or Comments by: None. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-06. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Councilmember Ribble Questions or Comments by: Mayor Batos suggested that resolutions accepting plats be placed on the consent agenda in the future. Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-06. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (D) First Reading: Ordinance No. 2016-03 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Providing for Councilmember Compensation; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2016-03. Questions or Comments by: Mayor Batos. Public Comment: William Williams Charles Dauray Wayne Smith Village Council Regular Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes March 16, 2016 Page 3 of6 Jim Sheehy Vicki Olson Don Eslick, ECCL Motion: Move to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2016-03 and set second reading for April 6, 2016. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Councilmember Ribble Action: Approved first reading of Ordinance No. 2016-03 and set second reading for April 6, 2016. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (E) Update on Village Center Planning Process Village Manager Sarkozy provided background information and explanation, and noted that the intent was to submit some revisions to the Comprehensive Plan and Framework Plan. Community Development Director Gibbs provided explanation of Framework Plan map revisions; vested rights language revisions; land use category renamed from Central Estero to Village Center; and density ranges increased for each Tier. She indicated that the Planning and Zoning Board would be reviewing and making a recommendation to Council and that the transmittal hearing had been scheduled before the Village Council for March 30. Questions or Comments by: Mayor Batos, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Councilmember Errington. Comments addressed concerns received regarding form-based code. Vice Mayor Levitan provided an explanation regarding comprehensive plan, land use map, and density. Public Comment: Don Eslick, ECCL (F) Raptor Bay Update Community Development Director Gibbs spoke to the question raised at a recent Council meeting whether the Pelican Landing DRI was subject to jurisdiction of the Village. A memorandum from Nancy Stroud, Land Use Counsel, indicated that the DRI was subject to Village jurisdiction by virtue of Charter legislation and that the developer must submit to the Village an application to amend the golf course open space to residential development. Village Council Regular Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 16, 2016 Page 4 of6 Motion: Move that staff prepare at letter for builder WCI, to inform them that they need to come before Estero' s Planning and Zoning Board, as Estero has jurisdiction with their DRI. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan and Village Attorney Saunders. Public Comment: None. Action: Directed staff to prepare a letter for builder WCI, to inform them that they need to come before Estero' s Planning and Zoning Board, as Estero has jurisdiction with their DRI. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 8. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Appearance by: Ronald Chaney, Broadway A venue W resident, addressed concern regarding a ditch and culvert that was not being kept clear. Community Development Director Gibbs indicated that Code Enforcement would review the situation. 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble provided an update regarding the furniture in the new Village Hall. He also spoke regarding the second-hand chairs that had been acquired; there were 20 extra chairs that he suggested donating to a 501(c)(3) organization. He noted that an open house event for the new Village Hall had been scheduled for April 13, 2016. Motion: Move to direct staff to find a 501(c)(3) organization and donate the 20 extra chairs. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Directed staff to find a 501(c)(3) organization and donate the 20 extra chairs. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Vice Mayor Levitan conveyed appreciation to Councilmember Ribble on his efforts in making the new Village Hall a showplace for the community. Village Council Regular Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 16, 2016 Page 5 of6 Councilmember Errington provided reports regarding meetings and events attended, including a half-day event regarding open government and sunshine laws. She also suggested inviting representatives from the Conservancy of Southwest Florida, as well as Jim Beever, SW Florida Regional Planning Council, to upcoming Council meetings to present information regarding Lake Okeechobee and red tide. Councilmember Boesch reported that he met with residents regarding the community entrance to Villa Palmeras, and indicated that the residents would need to address their concerns to Commissioner Kiker, as Three Oaks Parkway was a County road. He also spoke regarding Bills that were recently passed by the Legislature. Councilmember Wilson spoke regarding a list, published in the News Press, oflocal projects in the proposed State budget that he will provide to the Village Attorney and Village Manager. He offered congratulations to the newly elected officials in Bonita Springs and Fort Myers Beach. He also noted that he was continuing to meet with the East Corkscrew Alliance. Mayor Batos reported that he met with approximately six groups, and had another six scheduled, regarding the state of the Village. He announced that he accepted, on behalf of the entire Council, the Paul Harris Fellow Award from the Rotary Club. 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS: Village Attorney Saunders stated that he planned to provide a detailed report regarding legislative issues in a few weeks. He also spoke to the possibility of two conflicts that he would be requesting a waiver: Rusty's Raw Bar and Grill and a vacation of a roadway easement. Brief discussion followed. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS: Village Manager Sarkozy provided an update regarding upcoming agenda items, including interviewing the Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board applicants. He noted that the next Council meeting would be in the Council Chambers of the new Village Hall. A motion to adjourn was duly made and seconded. 12.
