title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2017-09-20
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL NOVEMBER 15, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village Council Meeting Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 September 20, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Boesch Invocation: Offered by Mayor Boesch Roll Call: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. Village Manager Sarkozy spoke regarding the recent flooding as well as Hurricane Irma. He offered appreciation to Carol Sacco, Executive Assistant to the Village Manager, Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager, Randy Brodersen, Public Works, and commended the Councilmembers for their assistance to residents in their Districts. He addressed the declaration of an emergency in the Village, and noted the governmental process that took place in an effort to take care of issues in the Village. Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager, offered a PowerPoint Presentation entitled Hurricane Irma - Preliminary Debriefing, which included preliminary damage assessments; concentration of damage; damage assessment guidelines; preliminary totals; minor and major damage; Estero Parkway pipe cleaning; railroad, Cascades, and Belle Lago/The Reserve ditch cleaning; value of the Emergency Operations Center; Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 1 of9 communication improvements; and critical technology. He also shared a brief video featuring the east side of The Cascades community. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, Errington, Wilson, McLain, Batos, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included the requirements between minor and major damages; comments related to septic, flooding, lines lying in water; Rotary Club efforts; information provided on the Village website; pre-planning efforts of the Emergency Operations Center and the Estero Fire Rescue District; communications through cell phone service; importance of CodeRED; Xfinity hotspots; cooperation with ECCL; communications to the HOA presidents; appreciation to Maryann Batlle, Naples Daily News reporter; compliments to Marilyn Edwards' communications efforts; appreciation to the Village Manager and the staff; questions regarding Halfway Creek and Spring Creek; water flow from the east; development in Lee County; temporarily slowing down development in Estero until the Village Stormwater Master Plan becomes available. Councilmember Batos spoke regarding information that he provided to the Village Manager in an effort to obtain email, cell and telephone numbers from residents to enable the Village to provide important information to residents, particularly in emergency situations. He believed that the response rate would be greatest if this was done within the next week or two. Discussion ensued; there was Council consensus to move forward with Councilmember Batos' suggestion. Motion: Move to authorize Councilmember Levitan to attend the upcoming Board of Lee County Commissioners meeting and provide verbal input regarding development in the DRGR. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Mayor Boesch Action: Authorized Councilmember Levitan to attend the upcoming Board of Lee County Commissioners meeting and provide verbal input regarding development in the DRGR. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda with the addition of Council Liaison Assignments as agenda item 5 (f). Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Vice Mayor Ribble Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 2 of9 Action: Approved the agenda with the addition of Council Liaison Assignments as agenda item 5 (f). Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Gary Israel, Stoneybrook, on behalf of Rotary Clubs of District 6960, disaster relief efforts. Angele Nadeau, The Reserve at Estero, standing water and drainage issues. Barbara Saxton, The Reserve at Estero, representing Friends of River Oaks Park, opportunity to purchase a piece of property to assist with water management. Barry Freedman, The Vines, appreciation for direct contact from the Village Manager as well as Estero Fire District; question regarding how to deal with the railroad system and tree removal; and suggestion that Council pass a moratorium on building. Lou Frattarelli, Preserve at Corkscrew, water flow. Don Eslick, ECCL, Village engagement with development in the DR/GR. Patty Whitehead, Old Estero, applying pressure on Lee County and the State regarding development in the DR/GR; concern regarding Three Oaks treatment plant; debris along Tanglewood Lane and question regarding timeline of claw trucks coming to Estero for hurricane debris removal. Doug Saxton, The Reserve, communications via Facebook during power outage; water flow at The Reserve and an upcoming town hall meeting. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Errington offered appreciation for Rotarians. Councilmember Ribble suggested that Congressman Rooney's office become involved with the railroad issues. Councilmember Levitan noted that the Board of County Commissioners meeting that he agreed to attend actually occurred this morning. Councilmember McLain stated that he would be attending a town hall meeting at The Reserve. The meeting went into recess at 11:30 a.m. and reconvened at 11:41. 4. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Financial Report for July 31, 2017 Village Finance Director Roberson spoke to the extension issued by the Governor, due to the hurricane, for the budget hearings; however, the County Tax Collector asked that the Village conduct the hearings as soon as possible. Tentative budget hearings dates: September 26, 5:30 p.m.; final public hearing date: October 4, 5:30 p.m. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 3 of9 Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Councilmember Levitan Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 5. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Estero Life Magazine Village Manager Sarkozy reviewed the previous "trial basis" approval, which has expired. He recommended approval of the placement of full-page articles in Estero Life Magazine, beginning with the October 1, 2017 issue through September 30, 2018. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Wilson, McLain, and Vice Mayor Ribble. Discussion included support of the recommendation; content provided by the Village; and magazine distribution due to the various zip codes in Estero. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve placement of full-page articles in Estero Life Magazine, beginning with the October 1, 2017 issue through September 30, 2018. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember McLain Action: Approved placement of full-page articles in Estero Life Magazine, beginning with the October 1, 2017 issue through September 30, 2018. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Request for Bid RFB 2017-05 for Village Mowing Village Manager Sarkozy reviewed that, when the Village took over the roads, many contracts were executed via piggy-backing; at that time, Council asked that staff create bids for the Village in order to obtain our own contracts. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan referred to a comment made in a memorandum from the Assistant Village Attorney Derek Rooney related to the Village bid procedure and suggested that, if there was a question regarding the Village bid procedure, the ordinance should be amended. Village Attorney Saunders responded that he would get together with the Village Manager to prepare an amendment. In response to Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 4 of9 an inquiry from Councilmember McLain, Assistant to the Village Manager Coleman provided an explanation of the Lee County contracts taken over by the Village. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the content of the bid package and authorize the advertisement for competitive solicitation of RFB 2017-05 Mowing for Village of Estero Roadways, Step One - Qualifications and Step Two - Pricing. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember Ribble Action: Approved the content of the bid package and authorized the advertisement for competitive solicitation ofRFB 2017-05 Mowing for Village ofEstero Roadways, Step One - Qualifications and Step Two - Pricing. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (c) Request for Bid RFB 2017-06 for Landscape Maintenance for Village ofEstero Roadways Village Manager Sarkozy explained that this bid was a similar situation as the request for bid RFB 2017-05 in agenda item 5 (b). Questions or Comments: None. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the content of the bid package and authorize the advertisement for competitive solicitation ofRFB 2017-06 Landscape Maintenance for Village of Estero Roadways, Step One - Qualifications and Step Two - Pricing. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Batos Action: Approved the content of the bid package and authorized the advertisement for competitive solicitation ofRFB 2017-05 Mowing for Village ofEstero Roadways, Step One - Qualifications and Step Two - Pricing. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 5 of9 (d) Request for Bid RFB 2017-07 for Village Miscellaneous Right-of-Way Work Village Manager Sarkozy explained that the purpose of this bid was to obtain contractors capable of performing sidewalk and sign repairs, litter pickup, etc., on an on-call basis; annual award with a term of one year with the option to renew for an additional one year period. Questions or Comments: None. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the content of the bid package and authorize the advertisement for competitive solicitation of RFB 2017-07 for Miscellaneous R.O.W. Work for the Village ofEstero Right-of-Ways. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Approved the content of the bid package and authorized the advertisement for competitive solicitation ofRFB 2017-07 for Miscellaneous R.O.W. Work for the Village of Estero Right-of-Ways. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (e) Approve Ranking of Consultants and Authorize the Contract Negotiations for CN 2017-01 Estero Parkway Roadway/Landscape Design & Estero Roadways Landscape Master Plan Village Manager Sarkozy reviewed Council's authorization for staff to follow through with the Estero Parkway Roadway/Landscape Design & Estero Roadways Landscape Master Plan process and provided details regarding the process; first step in the process is ranking of consultants and authorizing contract negotiations. Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief review regarding the August 21, 2017 Competitive Negotiations Committee meeting and the August 28, 2017 Evaluation Committee meeting. He noted that, after considerable discussion, the Competitive Negotiations Committee ranked four firms to be invited for an interview/presentation. At their August 28, 2017 meeting, the Evaluation Committee ranked the four firms, resulting in Agnoli, Barber & Brundage as the No. 1 firm. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan and McLain. Discussion included, with the recent storms, the possibility of other stormwater issues and whether the Village will need to do other things to carry the water; whether the Village will need to deal with the Estero Parkway swales; identifying crossings; installing culverts; installing larger structures to accommodate a future issue; in moving forward, the process will be interactive and meetings will be held for discussions. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 6 of9 Public Comment: Doug Saxton, The Reserve, stated this was a great step in getting Estero Parkway done, and addressed concern regarding pedestrians. Motion: Move to: (1) Concur with the ranking of consultants for CN 2017-01 Estero Parkway Roadway/ Landscape Design & Estero Roadways Landscape Master Plan as follows: Number 1 - Agnoli, Barber & Brundage; Number 2 - Kimley-Hom; Number 3 -T.Y. Lin; Number 4-DRMP; (2) Authorize staff to negotiate a contract with the Number 1 ranked firm, Agnoli, Barber & Brundage to provide professional consulting services for the roadway/landscape design for improvements to Estero Parkway. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Vice Mayor Ribble Action: (1) Concurred with the ranking of consultants for CN 2017-01 Estero Parkway Roadway/ Landscape Design & Estero Roadways Landscape Master Plan as follows: Number 1 - Agnoli, Barber & Brundage; Number 2 - Kimley-Hom; Number 3 -T.Y. Lin; Number 4-DRMP; (2) Authorized staff to negotiate a contract with the Number 1 ranked firm, Agnoli, Barber & Brundage to provide professional consulting services for the roadway/landscape design for improvements to Estero Parkway. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (f) Council Liaison Assignments Mayor Boesch inquired whether there were any Councilmember objections to suspend Council Rules and address Council liaison assignments. There were no objections. Questions or Comments: Mayor Boesch, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Councilmembers Errington, Levitan, and McLain. Discussion included the lack of a generator at Village Hall; the code for the windows and doors does not meet the requirements; District 3 liaison assignments: Events, Parks and Recreation, Stormwater. Councilmember Errington would be willing to give up median landscaping except for US 41 and she also wanted to retain the Boomer property under her assignments. Councilmember McLain inquired regarding sanitation districts. Councilmember Levitan believed that someone needed to be involved with lift stations. Public Comment: None. Action: Tabled until the next meeting. 6. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Gary Israel asked regarding a possible shelter for a citizen with a pet. Discussion followed related to shelters in Estero. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 7 of9 7. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Vice Mayor Ribble: Provided a brief report regarding the marijuana workshop at the Florida League of Cities conference. Addressed concern regarding conversations that took place at the August 2 Council meeting when he was out of town and attended the meeting via teleconference. He requested that an agenda item be added to the next Council meeting agenda related to Council conduct at meetings. Councilmember Levitan: Spoke regarding the August 2 Council meeting and believed that, in the heat of an argument, things can happen in meetings; all members are affected when there is a problem. He hoped that the issue could be resolved by an apology and that Council would make certain it doesn't happen again. He recommended that the Councilmember assigned to stormwater contact Jim Wallace regarding a suggestion he made related to a watershed issue. He also congratulated the Lee County Board of Commissioners on their purchase of Edison Farms. Councilmember McLain: Provided a brief report regarding the Florida League of Cities conference; noting that it was very enlightening. He also addressed Home Rule and provided an update regarding the Veterans Day event. Councilmember Errington: Inquired regarding the expiration of the ordinance related to temporary moratorium on cannabis dispensing businesses. Community Development Director Gibbs responded the expiration date is December 7, 2017 and stated that Assistant Village Attorney Rooney was working on a new ordinance. Councilmember Errington addressed a conversation she and the Mayor had at the September 6 special Council meeting and explained that she asked forgiveness of the Mayor due to the heated conversation at the August 2 meeting. Councilmember Wilson: Spoke briefly regarding Lee County's acquisition of Edison Farms. He also addressed his disappointment when he viewed the video recording of the August 2 Council meeting. Mayor Boesch: Commented that Governor Scott did an outstanding job regarding the hurricane. He inquired whether the Council believed it would be appropriate to ask FEMA to be here for a town hall meeting. Village Manager Sarkozy responded that he checked into this and found that FEMA was set up to attend 6 meetings for up to 1,000 people each. He said he would continue to see if a meeting could be set up in Estero. Another suggestion was to obtain the FEMA information to provide on the Village website and to make it available at Village Hall. Mayor Boesch said he was pleased regarding Lee County's acquisition of Edison Farms. 8. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Village Attorney Saunders stated that the Council should be proud of the staff for doing a wonderful job during the hurricane. He also congratulated the Councilmembers on their interaction with constituents. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 8 of9 9. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: Village Manager Sarkozy said he intended to bring back at the next Council meeting commendations regarding the staff, county, etc., due to the good work involved with Hurricane Irma. Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop 10. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Comprehensive Plan Update/Overview Village Manager Sarkozy introduced. Ben Smith, LaRue Planning and Laura DeJohn, Johnson Engineering, PowerPoint: Status Report; Elements: Future Land Use; Transportation; Housing; Infrastructure; Conservation and Coastal Management; Recreation and Open Space; Intergovernmental Coordination; Capital Improvements; Public School Facilities; and Next Steps. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, Wilson, Mayor Boesch and Vice Mayor Ribble. Public Comment: None. A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 11. ADJOURNMENT: 1:48 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: £ tUkj v(/ttV- Kathy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk James R. Boesch, Mayor Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 20, 2017 Page 9 of9

raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village "Clerk. APPROVED BY COUNCIL OCTOBER 5, 2016 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting September 21, 2016 5:30 p.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 5:30 p.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Jim Reho - Lamb of God Church Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, and Finance Director Lisa Pace. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Boesch Seconded by: Councilmember Ribble Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 1 of7 3. PROCLAMATIONS: (a) Congratulating Lee Memorial Health System on its Centennial Anniversary and Recognizing Contributions to the Community Mayor Batos read the proclamation and presented it to Nancy McGovern, Board Member, and Michael Nachef, Director of Government Relations, Lee Memorial Health System. Mayor Batos acknowledged and welcomed Cub Scout Pack 30. 4. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of August 31, 2016 Minutes (b) Approval of September 7, 2016 Minutes Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. PUBLIC HEARINGS: (a) Resolution No. 2016-28 A Resolution of the Village of Estero, Lee County, Florida, Adopting the Final Levying of Ad Valorem Taxes for the Village of Estero, Lee County, Florida, for Fiscal Year 2016-2017; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read Resolution No. 2016-28 in its entirety. Village Manager Sarkozy provided explanation regarding agenda items 5 (a) and (b), including: accolades to the Finance Director for achieving the Certificate of Achievement for Excellence in Financial Reporting (CAFR) award for the first Village financial audit; the year ahead; construction projects; and proposed property tax rate. In following through with the Council's promise related to "Government Lite" the Village Manager proposed amending the fiscal year 2016-2017 ad valorem operating millage rate for tax (calendar) year 2016 from .8004 to .7998 in Resolution No. 2016-28. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Boesch, Errington, Wilson, Brown, Ribble, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included the lowered tax rate and appreciation for the Council and dedicated staff. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 2 of7 Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-28, as amended. Motion by: Seconded by: All 7 members of the Village Council All 7 members of the Village Council Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-28, as amended. Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief statement. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-29. Questions or Comments by: Councilmember Wilson. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 3 of7 Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2016-11. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2016-12, as amended. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Adopted Ordinance No. 2016-12, as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 7. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Resolution No. 2016-24 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Supporting the Everglades for the Next Generation Act, HR 4667 Providing Emergency Funding for Repairs to the Herbert Hoover Dike, the Everglades Land Acquisition Act of 2016, and the Federal Partnership for Clean Water Land Acquisition Act of 2016; and Providing an Effective Date Note: This agenda item was continued at the August 17, 2016 meeting. Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-24. Mayor Batos noted that revisions supplied by James Evans, City of Sanibel Natural Resources Director, were included in the resolution. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 4 of7 Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-24. Motion by: Councilmember Boesch Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-24. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Letters to Congressman Curt Clawson and Senator Bill Nelson, Supporting Key Pieces of Legislation Note: This agenda item was continued at the August 17, 2016 meeting. Mayor Batos provided a brief statement regarding the letters. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to authorize the Mayor to sign letters to Congressman Curt Clawson and Senator Bill Nelson, supporting key pieces oflegislation. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Action: Authorized the Mayor to sign letters to Congressman Curt Clawson and Senator Bill Nelson, supporting key pieces oflegislation. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: (c) Resolution No. 2016-25 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Approving, for the Purposes of Section 14 7(F) of the Internal Revenue Code and for Purposes of Florida Inter-Governmental Cooperation, the Issuance by the Capital Trust Agency of its Senior Housing Revenue Bond Anticipation Notes (The Colonnade of Estero Project), Series 2016, in One or More Series, either Taxable or Tax-Exempt, or Both, Pursuant to a Plan of Finance, in an Aggregate Principal Amount not to Exceed $20,000,000, and Senior Housing Revenue Bonds (The Colonnade of Estero Project), Series 2016, in One or More Series, either Taxable or Tax-Exempt, or Both, Pursuant to a Plan of Finance, in an Aggregate Principal Amount not to Exceed $150,000,000, for the Purpose of Financing the Costs of Such Facility, as more Fully Described Herein; Affirming the Purpose and Function of VO ANS SW Florida Healthcare, Inc.; and Providing an Effective Date Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 5 of7 Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-25. Village Manager Sarkozy provided explanation regarding the project. Village Attorney Saunders referred to an email from Jennifer LaRocco, Gray Robinson, which indicated that the Village was simply assisting with financing by passing the resolution; he assured Council that there were no liabilities involved. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-25. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Councilmember Errington Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-25. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: ( d) Agreement for Election Services between the Village of Estero and Sharon Harrington, the Supervisor of Elections of Lee County, Florida Village Manager Sarkozy and Village Attorney Saunders spoke briefly regarding the revised agreement; further information was expected from the Supervisor of Elections. The agreement was continued to the October 5, 2016 agenda. Brief discussion followed regarding a "Q & A" prepared by the Village Attorney's office which was suggested as an addendum to the agreement; early voting; preparation of the resolution that is required pursuant to the agreement; and the possibility of moving the election date to the presidential preference date. Public Comment: None. 8. WORKSHOP ITEMS: None. 9. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Beverly MacNellis conveyed praise for Marilyn Edwards. 10. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Brief discussion regarding the Mayor's speech at the September 19, 2016 Donald Trump presidential campaign rally held at Germain Arena. 11. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 21, 2016 Page 6 of7 12. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 13. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice-Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Peter Lombardi, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 10:00 a.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE Mayor Batos led the Pledge of Allegiance. 3. ROLL CALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present. 4. APPROVAL OF AGENDA . Councilmember Errington moved approval of the agenda with a revision to the agenda by moving agenda item 5 (A) from the Council Workshop agenda as agenda item 5 (H) to this agenda, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously. 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) Presentation: Suzanne Bradach - Progress with the Lee Memorial Health System at Coconut Point expansion Suzanne Bradach, Director, Special Projects, Lee Memorial Health System, through a PowerPoint presentation, provided background information; vision and services for Coconut Point; team and commitment, including design and construction management team; new campus master plan and phase one multi-specialty building; maintaining communication and local community engagement; project schedule; and more to come: Bonita Bay Lifestyle Center; Healthy Life Center at Coconut Point Mall. Council discussion followed. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - September 23, 2015 : Page 1 of 5 (B) Resolution No. 2015-55 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero Florida Approving a Contract with Calvin, Giordano & Associates, Inc., to Provide Community Development Services to the Village of Estero; Authorizing the Village Mayor to Execute the Agreement; Authorizing the Village Manager to Expend Funds on Behalf of the Village; and Providing an Effective Date (Vice Mayor Levitan) The resolution title was read by the Village Clerk. Vice Mayor Levitan provided information regarding revisions. Through a PowerPoint presentation, he provided an explanation of how the amounts were derived in the contract, along with services included; fixed fee costs; estimated labor costs; labor multiplier; fixed fee calculation; comparison to Lee County proposal; fee revenue estimates; building services/IT fees; revenue estimate for other services; and other factors. Council discussion followed regarding recovering fees; number of staff that would be located in Estero; impact fees; parcel inventory; redevelopment; and IT services. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Ribble moved to adopt Resolution No. 2015-55, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (C) First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-12 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Establishing the Village of Estero Community Development Department; Providing a Severability Clause; Repealing and Rescinding all Ordinances in Conflict; and Providing an Effective Date (Vice Mayor Levitan) The ordinance title was read by the Village Clerk. Vice Mayor Levitan provided an explanation of the ordinance and indicated that the Village was required to create departments by ordinance. He addressed notice to Lee County and suggested that the Village collect fees immediately. He stated he believed that additional language was needed in Section 5 regarding effective date, to revise Section 5 to read: "This Ordinance shall be effective immediately upon adoption on second reading. Upon the effective date, all applications filed pursuant to the Land Development Code for properties within the boundaries of the Village of Estero shall be filed with the Estero Community Development Department." Mayor Batos inquired whether there were any objections to the revision; none were noted. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Ribble moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-12 with the revision to Section 5, seconded by Councilmember Wilson, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes -- September 23, 2015 Page 2 of 5 (D) First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-13 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Providing for the Recovery of Professional Fees and Costs. and Village Staff Costs for the Review of Certain Enumerated Development Applications and Procedures Therefore; Providing for Fixed Fees for the Village Review of Certain Enumerated Development Applications; Providing for Payment of Cost Recovery and Fixed Fees; Providing for Conflicts; Providing a Severability Clause; and Providing an Effective Date (Vice Mayor Levitan) The ordinance title was read by the Village Clerk. Vice Mayor Levitan provided an explanation of the ordinance and indicated that a fee schedule would follow with a resolution on the October 7, 2015 Council agenda. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Errington moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-13, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (E) First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-14 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Adopting Chapter 162, Florida Statutes, Providing for Municipal Code Enforcement; Establishing Enforcement Procedures and Authorities; Amending Conflicting Provisions of Code; Providing for Codification; Providing for Conflict with other Ordinances; Providing for Severability; and Providing for an Effective Date (Vice Mayor Levitan) The ordinance title was read by the Village Clerk. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Ribble moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-14, seconded by Councilmember Brown. Discussion of the motion followed. Vice Mayor Levitan believed that there may be revisions to the ordinance prior to second reading. The motion was called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (F) Second Reading and Public Hearing: Ordinance No. 2015-11 An Emergency Ordinance of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Village of Estero Ordinance 2015-08, Declaring an Emergency; Amending Village of Estero Land Development Code Chapters 6 & 10 to Adopt Flood Hazard Maps, Designate a Floodplain Administrator, Adopt Procedures and Criteria for Development in Flood Hazard Areas, and for Other Purposes; Adopt Technical Amendments to the Florida Building Code; Provide for Conflicts of Law, Severability, Codification and Scriveners Errors, Modifications that may arise from Consideration at Public Hearing and an Effective Date (Village Attorney) The ordinance title was read by the Village Clerk. The public hearing was opened. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - September 23, 2015 Page 3 of 5 Village Attorney Saunders provided an explanation of the ordinance and the reason for the emergency language. Mayor Batos called for public comment, no one came forward, and the public hearing was closed. Councilmember Ribble moved to declare an emergency and adopt Ordinance No. 2015-11, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried through a roll call vote. (G) _ Resolution No. 2015-67 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero Florida Establishing Meeting Dates, Time, and Location for Meetings and Workshops of the Village of Estero for 2016 The resolution title was read by the Village Clerk. Village Manager Lombardi referred to the schedule, noted the dates that the Fire District meeting room would not be available for use, and noted the dates providing for a summer break as well as Thanksgiving and Christmas. Discussion followed regarding reserving the room at the Estero Recreation Center to accommodate the meetings when the Fire District meeting room was not available. It was also noted that all meetings would begin at 10:00 a.m. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Wilson moved to adopt Resolution No. 2015-67, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (H) = Short-term Lease with The Brooks Executive Suites for Community Development Department Offices (Councilmember Ribble) Councilmember Ribble provided background information regarding the lease, noting that the property location was 9990 Coconut Road; the lease would cover 7 offices; however, there was room for expansion. Village Manager Lombardi spoke to the specifics of the lease. Discussion followed regarding computer servers; the automatic 6-month renewal of the lease; and Monday through Friday use of the building. Village Attorney Saunders stated that, in terms of approval of the lease, a motion to adopt a resolution to approve the lease would be needed. Village Attorney Saunders noted that he would prepare a simple resolution for the Mayor's signature that would be dated today. Regarding the renewal of the lease, he stated that it would be advisable to suggest a change to the language indicating a month-to-month lease after the 6-month term to avoid confusion. Discussion ensued regarding changing the begin date of the lease from September 1, 2015 to October 1, 2015, running through March 31, 2016. Village Attorney Saunders added that wording would be added that if the lease was terminated by the Village, the Village would then be on a'month to month lease. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Vice Mayor Levitan moved to approve a resolution that will be drawn up this afternoon to be signed by the Mayor as well as approving the Mayor's signature on the lease, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes ~ September 23, 2015 Page 4 of 5 6, PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Mayor Batos called for public input on any issue and no one came forward. 7. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS None. 8. VILLAGE MANAGER'S COMMENTS None. 9, VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S COMMENTS None. 10. ADJOURN Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 11:50 a.m. and noted that the Workshop would follow. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF EST. , FLORIDA By: Katt By: Kathy Hall, MMC Nicholas Batos Village Clerk Mayor Minutes approved as presented: October 7, 2015 Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - September 23, 2015 Page 5 of 5 VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP OF SEPTEMBER 23, 2015 The Village of Estero Council Workshop was held on this date at the Estero Fire Rescue District Meeting Room, 21500 Three Oaks Parkway, Estero, Florida. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice-Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. - Also present: Village Manager Peter Lombardi, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 12:01 p.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE The Pledge of Allegiance was conducted at the preceding Special Council meeting. 3. ROLL CALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present. 4, APPROVAL OF AGENDA A motion to approve the agenda was not made. 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) _ Discussion: Short-term Lease with The Brooks Executive Suites for Community Development Department Offices (Councilmember Ribble) Agenda item was moved to the preceding Special Council meeting agenda for action. (B) _ Discussion: Obtaining a Lobbyist (Mayor Batos) Village Attorney Saunders stated that it would be a good idea for the Village to have a voice in Tallahassee and believed that all the cities and counties in the southwest Florida region had representation. He noted that there were no major issues affecting Estero; however, there may be local bills and funding opportunities. He stated that, if the Council was interested in retaining Gray Robinson with him as the Village's lobbyist, he would charge a low fee for the next 6 to 8 months until the next legislative session began and then go on from there, on a month to month basis. He suggested an agreement effective November 1, 2015 at $1,500 per month, in effect until the end of August 2016; beginning September 1, 2016 the fee would increase to $2,500 per month. Brief Council discussion followed. Consensus was for Village Attorney Saunders to provide a resolution and an agreement at a future Council meeting. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Village Council Workshop Minutes - September 23, 2015 Page 1 of 3 (C) _ Discussion: 5-Year Capital Budget Plan (Mayor Batos) Mayor Batos provided an explanation regarding a proposed Capital Improvement Program procedure, along with a Research Service Agreement between the Village and Florida Gulf Coast University Board of Trustees to perform an Estero infrastructure survey. Council discussion followed. Public Comment: Doug Saxton, The Reserve resident, offered support for the process, hoped that the Village would get enough people to provide comments, and offered help as a volunteer. Mayor Batos inquired whether Councilmembers had revisions to the procedure. Brief discussion ensued and consensus was for the Village Manager to put the procedure and the Research Service Agreement on the next meeting agenda for approval. 9. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Mayor Batos called for public input on any issue and no one came forward. 10. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS Vice Mayor Levitan reported that a second meeting was held with the team of developers regarding the proposed Bill Spikowski Comprehensive Plan amendments. He indicated that the developers provided a detailed plan for consideration and a workshop with Seth Harry and Bill Spikowski would be scheduled. He also spoke regarding the recent Planning and Zoning Board meeting related to the Bella Terra development and the Corkscrew Road concerns raised by the public attending the meeting. Discussion followed regarding the Village conducting a traffic study due to upcoming projects that the Village would be commenting on. It was noted that three traffic study proposals were available for consideration. Village Manager Lombardi stated that copies of the three proposals would be provided and discussion scheduled for the next Council workshop. Councilmember Brown extended appreciation to Vice Mayor Levitan for his work on the Community Development Department contract and Councilmember Ribble for his work on the offices. Councilmember Errington noted that the Koreshan Preserve was looking really good. She also requested that the Council begin thinking about adopting an official Village seal and Village flag. Vice Mayor Levitan noted that there was an old Estero seal. Councilmember Wilson spoke regarding concerns raised at the Planning and Zoning Board meeting related to vehicles attempting to exit Bella Terra in the morning. 11, VILLAGE MANAGER'S COMMENTS Village Manager Lombardi spoke to the detailed plan on creating the Community Development Department that was presented by the Vice Mayor. Village Council Workshop Minutes - September 23, 2015 Page 2 of 3 12. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S COMMENTS None. 13. ADJOURN Councilmember Brown moved to adjourn the meeting, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried unanimously. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 12:37 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: athy hi By: Kathy Hall, MMC Nicholas Batos Village Clerk Mayor Minutes approved as presented: October 7, 2015 Village Council Workshop Minutes - September 23, 2015 Page 3 of 3

title: Budget Hearing Minutes - 2017-09-26
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL NOVEMBER 15, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village Council Special Meeting Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 September 26, 2017 5:30 p.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 5:31 p.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Boesch Invocation: Offered by Pastor James Reho from Lamb of God Lutheran Episcopal Church Roll Call: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6 (via teleconference), and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. Motion: Move to approve Councilmember Batos to participate in the meeting via teleconference. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Approved Councilmember Batos to participate in the meeting via teleconference. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 26,2017 Page 1 of6 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move that Agenda Item lO(a) - Council Rules of Procedure Workshop be moved to Agenda Item 5(c) under Action Items. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Councilmember Levitan Action: Agenda Item l0(a) - Council Rules of Procedure Workshop was moved to Agenda Item 5( c) under Action Items. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Motion: Move to approve the agenda as amended. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember McLain Action: Approved the agenda as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Gary Green, Breckenridge, spoke to a letter he presented to Council supporting Barry Freedman's comments related to the aftermath of issues his community is dealing with, as well as Council considering at least an emergency moratorium on approving all commercial development until the Comprehensive Plan has been approved and all traffic and other studies have been released. Garth Errington, Breckenridge, spoke regarding a Master Association's meeting that he attended recently that addressed how to be better prepared for the future; offered compliments to the Village Manager, staff, and Councilmembers that worked hard during Hurricane Irma; a wish for more public attendance and public comments at Village Council meetings; and the opportunity provided to the citizens to view live-streamed meetings online. Judy Freedman, The Vines, spoke regarding attendance at the recent Friends of the River Oaks Park Preserve event. Barry Freedman, The Vines, spoke to the possibility of the Village purchasing River Oaks Park; offered appreciation to the Village Manager and staff regarding speaking with railroad officials. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 26, 2017 Page 2 of6 4. PUBLIC HEARINGS: (a) Resolution No. 2017-24 A Resolution of the Village ofEstero, Lee County, Florida, Adopting the Tentative Levying of Ad Valorem Taxes for the Village of Estero, Lee County, Florida, for Fiscal Year 2017-2018; Establishing the Date, Time and Place for a Final Hearing to Adopt the Final Ad Valorem Taxes for Fiscal Year 2017-2018; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing. Resolution No. 2017-24 was read in its entirety by Village Clerk Hall. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the resolution and brief discussion followed. Second and final hearing will be held on October 4, 2017. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Wilson inquired regarding costs for hurricane cleanup; Village Manager Sarkozy responded that the Village probably would not have that information for approximately six months. Mayor Boesch noted that the Village had the lowest tax rate in Lee County, at 0.7998 mills. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-24. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-24. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2017-25 A Resolution of the Village of Estero, Lee County, Florida, Adopting the Tentative Budget for Fiscal Year 2017-2018; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing. Resolution No. 2017-25 was read in its entirety by Village Clerk Hall. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the resolution. Second and final hearing will be held on October 4, 2017. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-25. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 26, 2017 Page 3 of6 Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-25. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 5. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Approval oflnterlocal Agreement with Lee County for Natural Resources Services Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the Interlocal Agreement proposed by Lee County to provide Natural Resources municipal services for fiscal year 2017- 2018; service would continue through September 30, 2018. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers McLain, Levitan, and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included an inquiry regarding what the specific expectations were; the agreement includes the option to end services with a 60-day written notice and staff was urged to move forward at the earliest convenience. Mayor Boesch suggested, for the purposes of the budget, to accept the agreement and the topic could be brought back to Council in the future. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the Interlocal Agreement with Lee County for Natural Resources Services. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Councilmember Levitan Action: Approved the Interlocal Agreement with Lee County for Natural Resources Services. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Village Council Liaison Assignments to Outside Organizations and Areas of Strategic Concern Councilmember Errington stated that she was unhappy that liaison to the Koreshan State Park was taken away from her. She added that, regarding US 41, she wants to be able to be involved with future discussions regarding the maintenance of the sides of US 41 (shoulders, ditches, etc.). It was noted that an FDOT workshop regarding the medians would probably be scheduled later in October. On the liaison assignments list, the word "medians" will be deleted from US 41 and Estero Parkway. Councilmember Batos spoke regarding the DR/GR and believed that all Councilmembers should be open to discuss. He also asked for clarification regarding "open space" in his liaison assignment column and that it should be under parks and recreation; purchasing of land should also be all Council and not just one person. Mayor Boesch responded to DRGR comment regarding Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 26, 2017 Page 4 of6 the reason why one person should be designated and not several. Vice Mayor Ribble spoke regarding a meeting with the Village Manager related to approaching Congressman Rooney regarding the railway. He believed that it would be best for Councilmember Batos to be the point person for that topic; Councilmember Batos agreed to be the point person. Regarding open space, the Mayor and Councilmember McLain will decide. Public Comment: Beverly MacNellis, Marsh Landing Motion: Move to adopt the list of Liaison Assignments to Outside Organizations and Areas of Strategic Concern as amended. Motion by: Councilmember McLain Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Action: Adopted the list of Liaison Assignments to Outside Organizations and Areas of Strategic Concern as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: ( c) Council Rules of Procedure Vice Mayor Ribble reviewed that he requested this topic be placed on the agenda due to a concern regarding what he believed to be professional behavior issues that took place at the August 2 Council meeting while he was attending the meeting via teleconference in a public space. He stated that the Council Rules are clear on how Councilmembers are to handle themselves. He added that he spoke with the Village Attorney, who understood an apology would be provided to the Council. Councilmember Levitan believed that, in the heat of an argument, things can happen in meetings; all members are affected when there is a problem and it demeans the Council. He believed that a simple apology should be given to the Council. Councilmember Errington responded that maybe she did get excited; this keeps festering and she believed that the Council should let it go. She also referred to Councilmember Brown's treatment by some members of the Council and the fact that he was never apologized to. Councilmember Errington believed that she did not offend the Council and that an apology was not needed. She noted that she and the Mayor discussed this and that she had asked his forgiveness. She also believed that this should not be part of today's agenda. 6. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Patty Whitehead, Old Estero, spoke regarding Lee County delaying development orders and suggested writing a letter to the state. 7. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Vice Mayor Ribble: (1) Letters from constituents pertaining to failing septic systems. He asked that this topic be scheduled as a workshop. (2) At the Florida League of Cities conference he spoke with representatives of cities that have medical marijuana Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - September 26, 2017 Page 5 of6 distribution centers being developed. (3) The Elks Club is counting on Councilmembers to attend the breakfast on the morning of the Veterans Day event. Councilmember McLain: (1) Veterans Day event - lunch will be provided for all attendees; he appealed to all persons attending the Council meeting or viewing/listening online that it is important to honor all veterans. He wants to reach out to 5 to 10 veterans to stand up at the event and talk about their bios. (2) Being proactive with flooding issues. Councilmember Errington: (1) It was reported to her that there was debris in Estero River and the river had sustained some damage to the shorelines. (2) She requested that the Village Council ethics training be moved from FGCU and she wanted the public to be invited as well as live-streaming the training. title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2017-10-04
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL DECEMBER 6, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village Council Meeting Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 October 4, 2017 5:30 p.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 5:30 p.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Boesch Invocation: Pastor Nicholas Weston from River of Life Assembly of God Roll Call: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Development Review Manager Walter McCarthy 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Batos Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 1 of8 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Bill Carr, Riverwoods Plantation, Council Rules of Procedure as related to impropriety of Design Review Board and Planning and Zoning Board. 4. PUBLIC HEARINGS: (a) Resolution No. 2017-27 A Resolution of the Village of Estero, Florida, Adopting the Final Levying of Ad Valorem Taxes for the Village of Estero, Lee County, Florida, for Fiscal Year 2017-2018; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing and Resolution No. 2017-27 was read in its entirety by Village Clerk Hall. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the resolution, and noted that the budget has been subject to public review; there have been no comments received. He suggested that Council drop the tax rate by 2 cents, to 0. 7798 Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-27 as amended, with a final ad valorem operating millage rate of 0.7798 mills. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Councilmember Wilson Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-27 as amended, with a final ad valorem operating millage rate of 0. 7798 mills, at 5:39 p.m. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2017-28 A Resolution of the Village of Estero, Florida, Adopting the Final Budget for Fiscal Year 2017-2018; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing and Resolution No. 2017-28 was read in its entirety by Village Clerk Hall. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the resolution and noted that the budget provides plenty of latitude and flexibility as the Council sees fit. He also spoke regarding costs due to Hurricane Irma. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Batos. Public Comment: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 2 of8 Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-28. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-28, at 5:43 p.m. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 5. ORDINANCE SECOND READINGS AND PUBLIC HEARINGS: (a) Ordinance No. 2017-07 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Establishing Procedures for the Declaration of a State of Local Emergency; Providing Definitions; Purpose and Intent; Authority to Declare a State of Local Emergency; Method of Declaring and Terminating a State of Local Emergency; Authority of Village Council; Effect of Declaration of a State of Local Emergency; Prohibitions During Declared State of Local Emergency; Access to Emergency Preparedness Fund; Penalties for Violations; Repeal of Prior Ordinances; Conflict and Severability; and Effective Date The title of Ordinance No. 2017-07 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Mayor Boesch asked whether the ordinance had been properly advertised; Village Clerk Hall responded affirmatively. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an overview of the ordinance. Assistant to the Village Manager, Kyle Coleman, explained why staff recommended that the ordinance be tabled to allow for review by experts. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to table Ordinance No. 2017-07 indefinitely. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Councilmember Wilson Action: Ordinance No. 2017-07 was tabled indefinitely. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Ordinance No. 2017-08 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Providing for and Approving the Authority of the Mayor to Extend Participation in Interlocal Agreement with Lee County for the Continuation of the University Landscaping Operation and Maintenance Special Improvement Unit to Provide an Enhanced Level of Landscaping along Certain Portions of Corkscrew Road, Estero Parkway and Ben Hill Griffin Parkway within the Village Limits; Providing for an Extended Term and an Effective Date Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 3 of8 The title of Ordinance No. 2017-08 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Mayor Boesch asked whether the ordinance had been properly advertised; Village Clerk Hall responded affirmatively. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the ordinance, and noted that this was on a year to year basis. He also noted that a request was received from the Tidewater residents to remove the assessment from the tax rolls and allow for the Homeowner's Association to pick up the cost rather than the individual lots; an amendment would be presented to the Council in the next several months. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, McLain, and Wilson. Discussion included whether Lee County would go along with the Homeowner's Association picking up the cost; landscaping impacts from the recent hurricane and whether downed trees would be replaced; obtaining a copy of the budget to make certain the Village portion was getting a good pro-rata share; brief discussion related to Grandezza, Miromar, and Gulf Coast Town Center. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2017-08. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Adopted Ordinance No. 2017-08. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 6. ORDINANCE FIRST READING: (a) Park Place of Estero (DCI2017-E002)-Amendment to Residential Planned Development Ordinance No. 2017-09 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Approving with Conditions an Amendment to a Residential Planned Development with Deviations to Allow a Reconfiguration of the Master Concept Plan for Property Located at 9400 Block Lane Consisting of Approximately 5 Acres; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Ordinance No. 2017-09 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Community Development Director Gibbs provided a brief explanation of the ordinance, a request for an amendment to a Residential Planned Development with deviations for a5-acre site located on Block Lane off River Ranch Road. The property was rezoned by Lee County for a residential subdivision with 26 single family lots; the applicant is amending the plan for 16 single family lots and a different configuration. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers McLain, Levitan, Errington, and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included the need for a drainage plan and road elevation information; buffer on the west side of the property; the lack of sidewalks; request for Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 4 of8 deviation is problematic; concern regarding heritage trees and large oak tree; maximum property coverage; density will be reduced substantially and will be better than what Lee County approved. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to pass first reading of Ordinance No. 2017-09. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Councilmember Errington Action: Passed first reading of Ordinance No. 2017-09. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 7. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Resolution No. 2017-29 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida Approving the University Highland Parcel IO Re-Plat; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2017-29 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Development Review Manager McCarthy provided an explanation of the request for replat to reconfigure tract IO at University Highland to create two tracts; Tract 1 0A containing 1.20 acres and Tract I OB containing 6.37 acres. It was noted that the Planning and Zoning Board recommended approval. Information provided by: David Hurst, Peninsula Engineering, provided an explanation regarding drainage the increased water storage by adding substantially to the lake. Al Moscato, UHLP, stated that their development would be beyond the standards and noted that a decision for the replat at this meeting does not allow going forward; there were additional steps to the process. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, McLain, Wilson, Errington, Vice Mayor Ribble and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included access from interior road; inquiry regarding flooding in Tiberon; concern regarding impervious surfaces; elevation of buildings and roads; stormwater management; concern regarding infrastructure; flooding from Grandezza; and recommendation from staff. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-29. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 5 of8 Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-29. Vote: (Roll call vote) Aye: Councilmembers Levitan, Batos, Wilson, and Mayor Boesch Nay: Councilmembers McLain, Errington, and Vice Mayor Ribble Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2017-30 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida Approving the City Mattress Re-Plat; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2017-29 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Development Review Manager McCarthy provided an explanation regarding the re-plat to reconfigure the common lot line between existing Lots 2A-1 and 2A-2 located within the Coconut Point Area 2 for construction of a retail furniture store on Lot 2A-1 next to City Mattress. Lot 2A-1 will be decreased in area from 2.14 acres to 1.74 acres and Lot 2A-2 will be increased in area from 1.23 acres to 1.63 acres. Lot 2A-2 is currently occupied by the City Mattress Building and Lot 2A-1 is vacant. Information provided by: Brent Addison, Grady Minor Engineering, explained that the request was to move the line over on the existing two parcels. Walter Irmen, representing City Mattress, clarified that the green space was being increased. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, Batos, and McLain. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2017-29. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2017-29. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 8. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Peter Dersley, Grandezza, stormwater 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Vice Mayor Ribble: Provided information related to Weeks Fish Camp; a trip to Washington, D.C., including meetings with Senators Rubio and Nelson, through Florida League of Cities (working with National League of Cities); topics will cover FEMA Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 6 of8 obligations, review of a three year return of reimbursements for municipalities, and the National Flood Insurance Program. Councilmember Levitan: Addressed concern regarding the recent storms and flooding, along with the stormwater master plan that will not be completed, reviewed and adopted for quite some time. He requested that the topic of a moratorium be placed on the next available agenda. Councilmember McLain: Addressed lift stations in the communities that were on the edge of overflowing due to power outages during the recent hurricane and pump stations and electrical boxes that are in disrepair. He recommended that the Village work with Lee County and take the necessary steps to correct the issues. Councilmember Errington: Summarized a report prepared by Peggy Phillips regarding the status of the Estero River since the hurricane. She also requested that a workshop be scheduled to discuss the needs of the river. Councilmember Batos: Recalled that the Village was paying the county for natural resources that would include the Estero River. Report regarding Lee Health new construction. Councilmember Wilson: Thanked the Council for lowering the millage rate by 2 cents. 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: Village Manager Sarkozy spoke regarding 38 trees along Estero River that were knocked over as a result of the hurricane. Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop The meeting went into recess at 7:29 p.m. and reconvened at 7:38 p.m. 12. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Florida Department of Transportation (FDOT): I-75 and Corkscrew Interim Improvements Florida Department of Transportation (FDOT) representatives provided a presentation regarding pending improvements to the Interstate 75 interchange that will begin construction during the spring of 2019. These interim improvements are expected to provide a more efficient experience for Estero drivers by reducing traffic backups and keeping the interchange at an acceptable level of service through year 2029 or beyond. Questions or Comments: Mayor Boesch and Councilmembers Wilson and Levitan. Discussion included an inquiry whether they would be ready for the 2040 plan; an inquiry regarding other portions of the roadway that are under Lee County jurisdiction; swales that flow into the culverts and the importance of culvert maintenance. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 7 of8 Public Comment: JJ Basso, Grandezza Peter Dersley, Grandezza Robert King, Bella Terra, ECCL and East Corkscrew Alliance A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 13. ADJOURNMENT: 8:21 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: ~./JJ.4..- Kathy ikii:MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 4, 2017 Page 8 of8

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2016-10-05
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village "Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL OCTOBER 19, 2016 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting October 5, 2016 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Nolen Rollins from Legacy Church Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 ( excused absence). Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Pace, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda with the addition of a briefing regarding hurricane Matthew from Fire Chief Scott Vanderbrook, Estero Fire Rescue. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the agenda as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 5, 2016 Page 1 of5 Fire Chief Scott Vanderbrook, Estero Fire Rescue, provided an update on hurricane Matthew and encouraged everyone to have plans ready and to watch news reports for updated information. Brief discussion followed related to contacting the Estero communities to remind them to clean out gutters. 3. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of September 21, 2016 Council Meeting Minutes Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 4. ORDINANCES: None. 5. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Agreement for Election Services between the Village ofEstero and Sharon Harrington, the Supervisor of Elections of Lee County, Florida Village Attorney Saunders provided an update on the agreement and stated that it was ready for approval. He spoke briefly about the estimated and actual election costs. He noted that the "Q & A" provided at the previous Council meeting would be added to the agreement as an addendum. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Wilson, Errington, Boesch, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included early voting and vote by mail. Public Comment: Don Eslick, ECCL Motion: Move to approve the agreement for elections services with Sharon Harrington, the Supervisor of Elections of Lee County, Florida. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 5, 2016 Page 2 ofS Action: Approved the agreement for elections services with Sharon Harrington, the Supervisor of Elections of Lee County, Florida. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Traffic Study Consultant Agreement Note: This agenda item was discussed at the August 31, 2016 Council meeting; the Council requested several revisions, to add intersections and roadway segments, and to revise some standard contract provisions. These revisions have increased the cost by $4,917. Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction and Community Development Director Gibbs presented information regarding revisions to the contract previously addressed at the August 31, 2016 Council meeting. Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan and Councilmembers Boesch, Errington, and Ribble. Discussion included amending the agreement by substituting the left tum at Marsh Landing Blvd and US 41 to the left tum at Fountain Lakes Blvd and US 41; new state road concepts; and Coconut Road at US 41. Public Comment: None. Motion: Approve the agreement between the Village ofEstero and Kimley-Hom for the Village Traffic Study, as amended. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the agreement between the Village ofEstero and Kimley-Hom for the Village Traffic Study, as amended. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (c) Resolution No. 2016-30 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Authorizing the Waiving of Competitive Bidding Procedures and Authorizing the Village Manager to Execute a Contract for Public Works Services with JGucciardo Consulting LLC; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-30. Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the high level review by Mr. Gucciardo. He also spoke regarding the previous and current county maintenance agreements and noted the 60-day notice requirement to pull out of county services. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 5, 2016 Page 3 of5 Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Wilson, Ribble, Boesch, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included an amendment to the agreement to set a maximum dollar amount to the rates charged. Section 5 - Setting of Rates Charged would be amended to include "not to exceed $15,600 annually." Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-30 and amend Section 5 of the agreement to include "not to exceed $15,600 annually." Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Boesch Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-30 and amended Section 5 of the agreement to include "not to exceed $15,600 annually." Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call vote; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 6. UNFINISHED BUSINESS: None. 7. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council Membership Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation regarding the fee to participate. Questions or Comments by: Vice Mayor Levitan, Councilmembers Errington, Ribble, Boesch, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included how the decision was made last year to opt out; participation with the Estero Bay Agency on Bay Management; it may be beneficial to join this year; if the Village did not participate, the county would make the payment and the Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council remained funded. Public Comment: Patty Whitehead Community Development Director Gibbs offered assistance in obtaining further information to bring back to the next Council meeting. 8. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Appearance by: Carol Pikor, Breckenridge, street lights on US 41 Doug Saxton, The Reserve, design of streets in Village Center and including The Reserve in the Estero Fire District Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 5, 2016 Page 4 of5 Marian Rosencrans, Breckenridge, Estero Parkway lights 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble requested that staff provide a monthly update on code enforcement activities. Councilmember Errington reported regarding the Conservation 20/20 kickoff; spoke regarding an upcoming Bonita Springs ordinance related to marijuana; and addressed concern regarding storefronts in District 4. Councilmember Boesch reported that Billy Hattaway was leaving Florida Department of Transportation to take a position in Orlando. He asked Mr. Hattaway to make arrangements for someone from his staff to make a presentation before the Village Council to explain Florida Department of Transportation's approach to complete streets, as well as a representative involved with bicycle paths. Councilmember Wilson requested that staff provide a report covering proposed development. 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Village Attorney Saunders reported that his firm had drafted two ordinances related to medical marijuana - one being a short moratorium on dispensaries and the other on licensing. He stated that he would provide copies of the ordinances to the Village Manager today. Mayor Batos inquired whether an ordinance would need to be in place prior to the upcoming election and Mr. Saunders responded "no." Vice Mayor Levitan inquired regarding controlling public space when the Village did not own it. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: Village Manager Sarkozy spoke briefly regarding fiscal year end budget amendments that would be coming before Council at an upcoming meeting. He also reported that he met with the publisher of Estero Lifestyle Magazine (soon to be known as Estero Life) regarding the Village publishing information in the magazine. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 12. ADJOURNMENT: 10:40 a.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: , }(a__:t~LL(_ Kathy Hall,MMc, Village Clerk By,; ~:::Zif ::2".§L~:- Nicholas Batos, Mayor Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 5, 2016 Page 5 of5
