APPROVED BY COUNCIL DECEMBER 6, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 October 25, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Boesch Invocation: Pastor Todd Weston from River of Life Assembly of God Roll Call: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 2. ZONING ORDINANCE SECOND READING AND PUBLIC HEARING: (a) Park Place ofEstero -Amendment to Residential Planned Development Zoning Ordinance No. 2017-09 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Approving with Conditions an Amendment to a Residential Planned Development with Deviations to Allow a Reconfiguration of the Master Concept Plan for Property Located at 9400 Block Lane Consisting of Approximately 5 Acres; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 25, 2017 Page 1 of6 Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing and inquired whether the ordinance had been properly advertised; Clerk Hall responded affirmatively and read the title of Ordinance No. 2017-09. Mayor Boesch provided an explanation of the quasi-judicial procedures. Audience and staff intending to testify were sworn in by Clerk Hall. Disclosure of ex parte communications: Councilmember Levitan - met with the developer, Rick Losee, and received two emails from him. Conflicts of interest: None Letters or other written communications received for the record: None Community Development Director Gibbs reviewed information presented at the first reading of the ordinance on October 4, 2017. Request for an amendment to a Residential Planned Development with deviations for a5-acre site located on Block Lane off River Ranch Road. The property was rezoned by Lee County for a residential subdivision with 26 single family lots; the applicant is amending the plan for 16 single family lots and a different configuration. The Planning and Zoning Board recommended approval with conditions, however, denial of deviation 4; it is consistent with the surrounding densities; the site plan has improved from the previous site plan; one deviation for lot coverage for 50% instead of 55%. Community Development Director Gibbs continued that, at the October 4, 2017 meeting, Council requested additional information, which was reviewed by Director Gibbs. She noted that the applicant agreed to put a buffer on the west side of the property. Discussion followed related to the delay in receiving the PowerPoint Presentation. Motion: Move to waive Council Rules regarding timely receipt of the PowerPoint Presentation and allow this hearing to go forward. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Councilmember Wilson Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos and Levitan. Discussion included staff not scheduling second reading of ordinances until the applicant provides their PowerPoint Presentation or any other new materials to be considered. Action: Waived Council Rules regarding timely receipt of the PowerPoint Presentation and allowed this hearing to go forward. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Presentation/Information provided by: Kristina Johnson, P.E., JR Evans Engineering Richard Losee, P .E., Developer Scott Windham, Windham Studio PowerPoint Presentation: Project Location; Surrounding Uses; Existing and Proposed Master Concept Plan; Requested Deviations ( 4 which were presented to PZB); Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes- October 25, 2017 Page 2 of6 Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, McLain, and Batos. Discussion included an inquiry whether Kristina Johnson was working on the Village Stormwater Management Plan (she is not); deviations; width of the road; open space calculation and whether the covenants would incorporate wording about the open space requirement; deed restrictions; and lot coverage. Scott Windham spoke regarding the landscape plan and site features of heritage trees, lake, and shaded plaza. Discussion followed regarding concerns brought up at the October 5, 2017 first ordinance reading. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, Wilson, Errington, Batos, McLain, Vice Mayor Ribble and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included lot coverage enhancing the overall project; lack of internal sidewalk; heritage trees; elevation; on-site stormwater management system; inquiry regarding language in their documents regarding stormwater maintenance; inquiry whether flooding occurred during recent storms and whether the existing agricultural property was a wetland; a portion of the property is in a FEMA special flood hazard area, however, their stormwater management system has been designed to accommodate that; green space; curb along the lake; berm height; buffer; Deviation 7 and location of the lake. Further detailed discussion followed regarding stormwater and sidewalks. Mayor Boesch believed that the Village should retain a water expert to provide an opinion prior to moving forward with the ordinance. Motion: Move to hire a water consultant for this project and future projects, and to continue this hearing until a consultant can review the project and provide an opm1on. Motion by: Mayor Boesch Seconded by: Action: Motion died due to lack of a second. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, Wilson, and Vice Mayor Levitan. Discussion included the fact that water issues go way beyond one development and the need to look at how water feeds into the Village; it was not fair to hold up this development; the applicant proposed several additional conditions to justify their moving forward, including several elevation requirements; and a suggestion to re-draft the ordinance and move forward. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2017-09 as drafted with the changes that have been discussed today, to be enumerated. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Councilmember Batos Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 25, 2017 Page 3 of6 Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan, Ribble, and McLain. Discussion included the possibility of the Village adopting a moratorium in the near future and the potential ramifications involved. It was suggested that the ordinance be revised and brought back to the next Council meeting. Land Use Attorney Stroud stated that she had been taking notes in order to revise the ordinance and suggested that Council take a break in order for her to revise the ordinance to be brought back after the break. The meeting went into recess at 11:49 a.m. and reconvened at 12:10 p.m. Land Use Attorney Stroud reviewed the revisions to Ordinance No. 2017-09 as follows: Section 1 - The Village Council approves the amendment to Resolution Z-08-032 the Residential Planned Development District, with the following conditions and deviations. Section 2 - 1. The development of this project must be consistent with the one-page Master Concept Plan entitled, "Park Place ofEstero", Master Concept Plan stamped received August 22October 11, 2017. The development must comply with all of the requirements of the Land Development Code at the time of development order approval. 3. Buffers: As part of the local development order approval, the development order plans must demonstrate buffering consistent with the Master Concept Plan and the Land Development Code. A Type A landscape buffer will be provided along the western perimeter of the property. 6. Pattern Book: Development must comply with the design elements identified in the Park Place ofEstero Pattern Book dated July 2017 and stamped received July 28, October 11, 2017. 9. b. Deviations: Deviation (2) seeks relief from the LDC Section 10-719(a) 10-296(i), which requires that roads provide a 20-foot wide pavement area with 2 foot valley gutter to allow a2 foot curb and gutter on the section adjacent to the lake as shown on Exhibit D. private local streets with closed drainage to have a minimum 24 foot wide pavement area with 12 foot travel lanes. The applicant requested a 20 foot ·.vide pavement area with tv,o 10 foot travel lanes. The deviation is hereby APPROVED. d. Deviation (4) is withdrawn by the applicant. e. Deviation (5) seeks relief from LDC Section 34-695 which requires a maximum lot coverage of 45%. The applicant requested a maximum lot coverage of 55%. The deviation is hereby APPROVED for 50%. Lot coverage restriction must be included in the restrictive covenants for each lot. 10. The perimeter berm shall be set at 16.25 feet elevation and the 25 year, 3 day storm event. 11. The road elevations shall be set at the 25 year, 3 day storm event. The minimum first floor building elevation shall set at the 100 year, 3 day storm event plus 1 foot. 12. Home Owner Association documents will provide for drainage maintenance by the Home Owner Association. Revisions to Exhibits as follows: Exhibit B Master Concept Plan stamped "Received" August 22,October 11, 2017 Exhibit C Park Place ofEstero Pattern Book; dated July 2017 stamped "Received"--MY 2-8- October 11, 201 7 Exhibit D Deviation 2 illustration Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 25, 2017 Page 4 of6 Substitute Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2017-09 as revised with the stated amendments. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Amendment to Substitute Motion: Move to include a condition for sidewalks on both sides of the street. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Vice Mayor Ribble Questions or Comments: Councilmember Batos and Vice Mayor Ribble. Discussion included debate regarding requiring sidewalks within a gated community that did not affect Estero as a whole. Substitute Amendment to Substitute Motion: Move to include a condition for a one- sided sidewalk up to the cul-de-sac. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Levitan and Batos. Discussion included an inquiry regarding justifying the sidewalk on a safety concern, how much would be added as a safety factor by two sided versus one sided; and an inquiry regarding how much of a safety element would there be with a small community of 16 homes. Note: Councilmember Batos originally seconded the substitute amendment; however, decided to withdraw his second. Action on Substitute Amendment to Substitute Motion: Motion failed. Vote: (Roll call) Aye: Councilmembers McLain and Levitan Nay: Councilmembers Errington, Batos, Wilson, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Mayor Boesch Abstentions: Action on Amendment to Substitute Motion: Approved a condition that a sidewalk shall be provided along both sides of the street. Vote: Aye: (Roll call) Councilmembers Errington, Wilson, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Mayor Boesch Nay: Councilmembers Levitan, McLain and Batos Abstentions: Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 25, 2017 Page 5 of6 Action on Main Motion: Adopted Ordinance No. 2017-09 with revisions as stated and a condition that a sidewalk shall be provided along both sides of the street. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call) Nay: Abstentions: Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop 3. COMPREHENSIVE PLAN WORKSHOP: (a) Overview of Population Trends and Other Data Community Development Director Gibbs provided a brief introduction. Presentation/Information provided by: David Farmer, CEO, Metro Forecasting PowerPoint Presentation: Estero Population History; Recent Permitting History; Comp Plan and Land Use; Estero Today; Estero at Build-out; Balancing Land Uses; Vacancy Rate Trends; Seasonal Population; Logistic Curve; Estero's Logistic Curve; and Estero's Future. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Wilson, Levitan, and Batos. Discussion included utilizing land outside the Village limits for Comprehensive Plan purposes; demographics; increases of school age children in the next five years; seasonal rate; and low income housing industry. Public Comment: None. A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 4. ADJOURNMENT: 12:58 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ES TERO, FLORIDA By:~~~ Kathy~ MC, Village Clerk Village Council Zoning Hearing and Comprehensive Plan Workshop Final Action Agenda/Minutes - October 25, 2017 Page 6 of6

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2015-10-28
raw_text: VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP OF OCTOBER 28, 2015 The Village of Estero Council Workshop was held on this date at the Estero Fire Rescue District Meeting Room, 21500 Three Oaks Parkway, Estero, Florida. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice-Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Peter Lombardi, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 10:00 a.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE Mayor Batos led the Pledge of Allegiance. 3. ROLLCALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present. 4. APPROVAL OF AGENDA Councilmember Brown moved approval of the agenda, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried unanimously. 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) Conservancy of Southwest Florida - Estero River Presentation - April Olson April Olson, Growth Management Specialist, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, through a PowerPoint presentation, spoke regarding the Estero Bay Watershed; headwaters; flows; history of the river; historic groves of Estero; Estero Bay store; Pre-Development Conditions 1944, 1953, 1998; flow data; 1953 and 2007 Hydropattems; vegetation; wildlife; water quality; watershed problems; economic significance; trails and parks; and Conservancy recommendations. Council discussion followed. Public Comment: Charles Dauray provided a handout entitled Estero River Conditions East of US 41, and spoke regarding the Estero Historic Preservation Citizens Committee mission; the committee's focus on the Estero River; digitizing records; the delicate balance between preservation and private property rights; and water flows. Village Council Workshop Minutes - October 28, 2015 Page 1 of7 Genelle Grant, Board President, Happehatchee Center, spoke regarding the importance of preserving the land and offered the Center's cooperation and help to work with the Village. Holley Rauen, Board Vice President, Happehatchee Center, stated that they would like to partner with anyone that wants to save the river and to work on a task force. She also invited everyone to view a video on the Happehatchee website. Bob Lienesch, Shadow Wood resident, addressed the need for a land acquisition program to allow for better access to the river. Stephanie Miller, Rookery Pointe resident, spoke regarding the importance ofland acquisition. Don Eslick, ECCL, stated that he served on the zoning panel when Estero and the river were zoned and he believed that a 50 foot setback existed on the south side of the river from the top of the bank in order to accommodate a walking and biking path, and spoke to the importance of maintaining the access to the river. He also spoke regarding assisting with the Koreshan State Park long term plan update; voluntary easements from property owners along the river; and the ECCL's interest in participating in a task force. Charles Dauray noted that some of the most wonderful walking paths in South Florida were located at the Koreshan State Park and spoke regarding the possibility of an annual pass for Estero residents. Councilmember Wilson spoke regarding attending the Farmer's Market at the Koreshan State Park and recalled that there was no fee required. Jean Pryal, Vice President, Estero Historical Society, spoke regarding Mimi Straub, visionary to the Estero community. Councilmember Ribble spoke regarding the upcoming Halloween party at the Koreshan State Park. Mayor Batos expressed appreciation to April Olson for the presentation and to everyone that participated in the discussion. (B) Fracking Discussion Caitlin Weber, Policy Analyst, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, through a Power Point presentation, spoke regarding oil in Estero; mineral right ownership; extraction techniques; and surface impacts of concern. Ralf Brookes, Attorney, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, continuing with the Power Point presentation, spoke regarding legal aspects oflocal ordinances; well stimulation ordinance language; water wells are not captured; no permit for well stimulation; municipal authority to review drilling permits - Section 377.24 F.S.; other government actions; local government action in Florida - use of local zoning; proposed preemption in Village Council Workshop Minutes October 28, 2015 Page 2 of7 State Legislation - HB 191 and SB 318; and recommendations for well stimulation ordinance. Public Comment: Peter Cangialosi, ECCL, addressed concern regarding fracking and urged Council to adopt an ordinance. Phil Douglas, Lighthouse Bay resident, urged Council to adopt an ordinance immediately and asked that letters be written to Representative Rodrigues and Senator Richter. He also spoke regarding the low economic value of the oil that would come out of this area. Dr. Karen Dwyer and Dr. John Dwyer, Naples residents, addressed concerns regarding fracking and urged Council to adopt an ordinance. Clara Anne Graham, President, League of Women Voters of Lee County, addressed concern regarding fracking and urged Council to adopt an ordinance. Ray Judah, Florida COC, addressed concern regarding fracking and the proposed bills, and urged Council to adopt an ordinance. Ruth McGregor, Sierra Club Calusa Group, addressed concern regarding fracking and urged Council to adopt an ordinance. Patty Whitehead, Old Estero resident, spoke regarding fracking, the Constitution, Florida Statutes, HB 191 and SB 318. Greg Toth, Naples resident, agreed with adoption of a local ordinance and stated that the majority of Lee County wells were due east of Estero. He encouraged the Village Council to get involved with the County and the State in order to prevent fracking from happening east of Estero. Ms. Weber provided written comments from the public to add to the record. Councilmember Errington conveyed appreciation to the Conservancy for the inaugural Fracking Summit. She also spoke regarding information she gathered from research relating to fracking, read from notes that she prepared related to HB 191 and SB 318, and asked that an ordinance be placed on the November 4, 2015 Council agenda. Attorney Saunders spoke regarding Lee County's pre-emption from regulations of the oil industry; they rely on implied pre-emptions. He also read the pre-emption language from the proposed bills and addressed opposition. He also addressed the Village's Home Rule authority to adopt an ordinance banning fracking, which could be taken away if legislation passed; liability; the possibility of Burt Harris claims; and the options of passing an ordinance, resolution, or both. Discussion followed regarding the sample ordinance; the commitment from Earth Justice to help intervene and defend any ordinances; concern about non-conventional oil drilling; lack of information about the status of mineral rights in Estero and the means to obtain the information. Village Council Workshop Minutes - October 28, 2015 Page 3 of7 Council consensus was for staff to prepare an ordinance banning fracking and a resolution urging the Legislature to ban fracking and to preserve Home Rule; and the ordinance to be scheduled to allow for adoption prior to the end of 2015. In response to an inquiry regarding Representative Rodrigues, Attorney Saunders responded that he was unable to attend the workshop due to Special Session in Tallahassee; however, he had expressed an interest in being at the workshop. The Workshop went into recess at 12:17 and reconvened at 1:00 p.m. (C) Village Vision Presentation: Seth Harry and Bill Spikowski Vice Mayor Levitan, through a PowerPoint presentation entitled Estero Planning Initiative, provided an overview of what would be discussed along with information to assist in answering the questions why was Estero doing this and what the Village was trying to achieve. Topics in the presentation included: What is Estero? What do people identify with? Mission statement dated October 2014 from the Estero Community Plan; Community Plan: Policy 19.2.2; Wants and Needs Identified in the Village Center Areas; The Vision so Far; Overall Goal; and Tiered Weighted Incentive System. Utilizing a second PowerPoint presentation, Vice Mayor Levitan addressed the steps taken thus far: Community planning initiative, 2013-14; Zoning in progress resolution, April 2015; Planning contract with Spikowski/Harry, May 2015; Public meeting on interim Comprehensive Plan amendments, August 2015; Process paused to consider objections; and Meetings to discuss counter proposals including density incentive structure, October 2015. Continuing through the PowerPoint presentation, Seth Harry addressed the following topics: How is what's left different; Zoning Mismatch; Possible Anchors; Place-type Attributes; Complete Streets; Diversity of Residential Types Serving Different Housing and Care Needs; Street Networks; and Place-making Neighborhood Structure. Bill Spikowski continued with the PowerPoint presentation, addressing: Conventional Zoning; Planned Development Zoning; Recent adjustments for Village Center will create new categories on the future Land Use Map for the Village Center; categories will allow Village Council to authorize density bonuses for above and beyond commitments such as design, landscaping, river access, public spaces; a framework plan will ensure connectivity of streets and paths, replacing earlier idea for the Village to create conceptual regulating plans. Vice Mayor Levitan stated that there was a lot of work ahead to get from the conceptual idea. He spoke to working on a collaborative basis to reach as many of objectives as possible. He also spoke regarding the Comprehensive Plan. Public Comment: Jim Wallace, representing Genova, provided suggestions and urged the Council to continue the productive collaborative process. He made the following points: be certain that the menu of incentives was realistic; the program must represent the wants of the Village Council Workshop Minutes - October 28, 2015 Page 4 of7 Village but also what makes sense for the project involved; be pragmatic and find common ground in an expeditious way. Carolyn Mijokovic, Bella Terra resident, addressed concern regarding obtaining approval from Lee County. Mayor Batos responded that the Council would make the decisions. Ms. Mijokovic also inquired regarding "smart" streets. Howard Gutman and Katie Sproul from North Point, conveyed appreciation to Council regarding meeting with landowners and coming up with a good plan that was incentive based. Concern was addressed regarding creating the type of project that was economically feasible based on some of the things that Seth Harry presented. It was noted that they have sold over 3,000 units in Southwest Florida and only sold to two millennials. They also believed that flexibility and alternative options were needed. Greg Toth, land owner, spoke to economic feasibility, road network, water network, and greenspace. He noted that the present market place was residential and financing could not be obtained if a project included more than 25% commercial. Andy Djamoos, representing Village Partners, Estero on the River, comprising approximately 87 acres, cut into quadrants, abutting Estero River and railroad tracks. He addressed concern regarding creating a walkable community. He indicated support, noted that he had only been invited to one developer's meeting, and encouraged Council to let him know how he can get more involved. Sylvia Busch, Villa Palmeras resident, inquired whether Council had considered a budget for the Village Center. Mayor Batos responded that, at this point, it was premature due to the lack of a plan. Don Eslick, ECCL, spoke to the importance of being collaborative. He hoped that henceforth the process would be open to the public. He also spoke to the need for an assessment of all the things in the two areas, how the community could capitalize on the use, and having a sense of place. Glenna Rohrbach, Estero Art League, addressed concern regarding cultural aspects. Roger Strelow, Planning and Zoning Board, expressed optimism about how the Village was progressing and believed that the dialogue was encouraging. Al O'Donnell, Design Review Board, believed that incentive based was a good idea and spoke to creativity of developers. Hollie Rauen, spoke regarding environment; trees; quality of Estero River; and the challenge to developers. Doug Saxton, The Reserve resident, addressed his hope that the Village Center would be not only a place to live but a destination as well, and that part of the corridor be reserved for trails. Cliff Fleming, attorney representing developers, spoke regarding respect for the developers and preserving the community for the long term. Village Council Workshop Minutes- October 28, 2015 Page 5 of7 Patty Whitehead, Old Estero resident, addressed concern for a bikeable/walkable community in order to go from one amenity to the next. Councilmember Ribble addressed concern regarding the lack of information related to the meetings between the developers and Vice Mayor Levitan. He noted that he had spoken with a developer at the walkable communities' symposium as well as two developers at Village Hall. He was hopeful that, moving forward, more information would be shared. He also spoke regarding Joe Minicozzi, a presenter at the walkable communities' symposium; he would like to have the new Village Manager reach out to him to determine whether the Village was on the right track. Councilmember Brown noted that everyone had worked long and hard to become a community; the developers had invested a lot of money without a return; he hoped that the process could be expedited; and appreciated everyone's efforts. Councilmember Errington echoed Councilmember Ribble's comments regarding sharing information. She spoke regarding the idea of having a non-working rail system, which needed to be cleared up. She also noted that the northeast comer of US 41 and Corkscrew Road should be included in the plan and spoke regarding opportunities for several amenities. Councilmember Boesch agreed regarding reaching out to Mr. Minicozzi; was pleased that the developers were in attendance; and believed that a good attitude would bring about positive results. Councilmember Wilson believed he had a better understanding through the presentations and agreed regarding speaking to Mr. Minicozzi about coming to speak with the Council. Mayor Batos stated that the ideas brought forward were preliminary and that everything had to be successful for both sides. He encouraged the developers to reach out to each of the Councilmembers in order to get clarification of their ideas. 9. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Mayor Batos called for public input on any issue and no one came forward. 10. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS None. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S COMMENTS None. 12. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S COMMENTS None. Village Council Workshop Minutes - October 28, 2015 Page 6 of7 13. ADJOURN A motion to adjourn was duly made, called and carried unanimously. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 2:54 p.m. ATTEST: By: ....,ka:t:Nf~ , Kathy Hall, MMC Village Clerk Minutes approved as submitted: December 2, 2015 Village Council Workshop Minutes - October 28, 2015 VILL~;, FLORIDA By:_ ~~ Nicholas Batos Mayor Page 7 of7

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2017-11-01
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL JANUARY 3, 2018 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village Council Meeting Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 November 1, 2017 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Boesch Invocation: Pastor Mark Goodman from Christ Community Ministries Roll Call: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. Mayor Boesch referred to the three resolutions listed under agenda item 9 - Council Communications. Discussion ensued and consensus was to address the resolutions under that agenda item. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Mayor Boesch stated that he received a request from Councilmember Batos to add agenda item 6 ( c) Letter to President Trump regarding continued support for the repairs to the Herbert Hoover Dike and moving forward on the Everglades restoration projects. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 1, 2017 Page 1 of8 Motion: Move to approve the agenda as amended, adding agenda item 6 ( c) Letter to President Trump. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Approved the agenda as amended, adding agenda item 6 (c) Letter to President Trump. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 3. INTRODUCTION: Michael V. Martin Ph.D., President, Florida Gulf Coast University (FGCU) Vice Mayor Ribble provided an introduction. Dr. Martin shared his personal appreciation of the warm welcome to the area and spoke regarding FGCU's growth and the success of the students, community engagement, and expansion on the campus. 4. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Barbara Saxton, The Reserve, representing Friends of River Oaks Park, provided an update on the River Oaks Park project and requested that the Village hold a workshop in January. Patty Whitehead, Old Estero, reflected on some of the comments brought up by Dr. Martin. Questions or Comments: Mayor Boesch suggested that Ms. Saxton speak with the Village Manager to set up a workshop. 5. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Financial Report for September 30, 2017 (b) September 6, 2017 Council Meeting Minutes Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 1, 2017 Page 2 of8 6. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Stray Animal Control Services Interlocal Agreement with Lee County Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation of the agreement, which has been ongomg. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, and Errington. Discussion included Lee County providing a quarterly report; volume of stray animals reported; convenience of the service; and price. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the Interlocal Agreement with Lee County for Stray Animal Control Services. Motion by: Councilmember McLain Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Approved the Interlocal Agreement with Lee County for Stray Animal Control Services. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Amended 2018 Village of Estero Legislative Priorities Village Manager Sarkozy reviewed that the list was discussed at a workshop, the list was modified since that time, and after approval it would be provided to the Village Attorney. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, Batos, Wilson and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included how the extension of the Jeff Sommer Memorial Highway would be handled; and designation of Corkscrew Road, west of Ali co Road and east of the I-75 interchange, as a truck free highway. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the amended 2018 Village ofEstero Legislative Priorities. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved the amended 2018 Village of Estero Legislative Priorities. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 1, 2017 Page 3 of8 (c) Letter to President Trump Regarding Continued Support for the Repairs to the Herbert Hoover Dike and Moving Forward on the Everglades Restoration Projects Councilmember Batos provided an explanation regarding the request from Congressman Rooney asking that communities support a letter to President Trump regarding the Herbert Hoover Dyke project and the Everglades restoration projects. Motion: Move to approve a letter to President Trump regarding continued support for the repairs to the Herbert Hoover Dike and moving forward on the Everglades restoration projects, signed by the Mayor. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Approved a letter to President Trump regarding continued support for the repairs to the Herbert Hoover Dike and moving forward on the Everglades restoration projects, signed by the Mayor. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 7. ORDINANCE FIRST READING/PUBLIC HEARING: (a) Ordinance No. 2017-10 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Amending Chapter 34, Division 31 ofEstero Land Development Code to Prohibit Medical Marijuana Facilities within the Boundaries of the Village; Providing for a Moratorium Contingency; Providing for Codification, Severability, Conflicts and an Effective Date The title of Ordinance No. 2017-10 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Mayor Boesch inquired whether the ordinance had been properly advertised; Village Clerk Hall responded affirmatively; Village Attorney Saunders provided comments. Public Comment: Bill Carr, Carriage Court, Estero, supported the ordinance. Questions or Comments: Vice Mayor Ribble reviewed the third "Whereas" of the ordinance relating to population and expressed disappointment that the Planning and Zoning Board did not challenge testimony stating that a dispensary was not located nearby. Councilmember Batos stated that he received an email from 2 couples. Councilmember Levitan noted that there was an overwhelming amount of people who voted for medical marijuana; however, until the legislature acts, it was appropriate for the Council to ban dispensaries. He also addressed people in need. Motion: Move to pass first reading and schedule second reading/public hearing for November 15, 2017, at 9:30 a.m. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Councilmember Ribble Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 1, 2017 Page 4 of8 Action: Passed first reading and scheduled second reading/public hearing for November 15, 2017, at 9:30 a.m. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Ordinance No. 2017-11 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Further Amending Ordinance No. 15-01; Changing the Qualifications for Membership of the Design Review Board; Adding a New Section 4 Regarding Ethics and Conflicts of Interest; Revising the Quorum Requirement for the Design Review Board; Providing a Severability Clause; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch provided an introduction and the title of Ordinance No. 2017-10 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Village Manager Sarkozy addressed the Board membership and noted that the question has been how to deal with conflicts of interest. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos, Levitan, Errington, Vice Mayor Ribble, Wilson, and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included the risk of losing some of the professionals that have been in place for a long time; possibility of continuing to allow them to stay on the Board and when someone has a recusal they leave the room; there are many volunteers in the area; there are ways to set standards for board members' behavior; the Village has been widely criticized and Council has to do something to eliminate the appearance of impropriety. Public Comment: Gary Green, Pensacola A venue, addressed his appreciation to volunteer board members and his support for the ordinance. Bill Carr, Carriage Court, Estero, supported the ordinance. Patty Whitehead, Old Estero, speaking as a member of the Design Review Board and a member of the public, understood the perceived results of the public; suggested including retired planners or public officials on the board and having a balance between paid and non-paid board members. Motion: Move to pass first reading and schedule second reading/public hearing for November 15, 2017. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Levitan Councilmember Wilson Action: Passed first reading and scheduled second reading/public hearing for November 15, 2017. Vote: (Roll call vote) Aye: Councilmembers Levitan, McLain, Errington, Wilson, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Mayor Boesch Nay: Councilmember Batos Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November I, 2017 Page 5 of8 8. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Meredith Barnard, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, offered assistance from the Conservancy as a resource to the Council and provided information regarding an upcoming event on Wednesday, November 8. 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: (a) Discussion of three Resolutions as suggested by Councilmember Errington: (1) Supporting the Designation of the Coach Jeff Sommer Memorial Highway on U.S. 41 from the North Boundary to the South Boundary of the Village ofEstero (2) Supporting Senate Bill 8 and House Bill 21 Controlled Substances (3) Supporting Senate Bill 434 and House Bill 407 Creating the Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome Pilot Project Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Errington, Ribble, Batos, Levitan, Vice Mayor Ribble, and Mayor Boesch. Discussion included communications between Councilmember Errington and Mrs. Sommer; an inquiry why Councilmember Batos was not handling the extension of the Jeff Sommer Memorial Highway; support for Senator Benacquisto; letters to senators would be more appropriate than Council resolutions; Council's job was to work on local issues. Village Attorney Saunders noted that, related to the Senate and House Bills, there were things that could cause problems for some people; he recommended that the Village not take a position. Consensus was to bring back the resolution related to the designation of the Coach Jeff Sommer Memorial Highway on U.S. 41 on the Consent Agenda for the November 15, 2017 Council meeting. Councilmember Wilson: Spoke regarding the old list of suggested workshops and requested a workshop regarding the potential properties available. Councilmember Batos: Provided a list of workshops to the Village Manager. Councilmember Errington: Requested a workshop with Lee County Utilities. Councilmember McLain: Spoke regarding the funds for the Seminole Gulf Railroad project being re-positioned and comments that were solicited. He reported that 188 letters from his district would be submitted. Vice Mayor Ribble: Spoke regarding the Veterans Day events, including the Elks Club breakfast. 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Reported that he will be meeting with legislators soon. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November I, 2017 Page 6 of8 Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop The meeting went into recess at 11:35 a.m. and reconvened at 11:45 a.m. 12. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Comprehensive Plan: (1) Recreation and Open Space (2) Public School Facilities Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction and asked the Council to send all suggested Comprehensive Plan revisions to him. Community Development Director Gibbs reported that the November 8, 2017 Comprehensive Plan workshop had been postponed. Laura DeJohn, Johnson Engineering, Public School Facilities PowerPoint Presentation: Major Objectives - Location and Development, Facilities Coordination, Service Levels and Environmental Education and Outreach; Issues for Discussion and Decision. Christine Patricca, Lee County School Board Member, District 3, addressed emergency response, preparedness, shelters, and communications. Questions or Comments: Mayor Boesch expressed frustration with School Board communications. Councilmember Batos stated he hoped that an interlocal agreement would come forward before the end of the year. Councilmember Levitan asked Ms. Patricca to provide general comments on policies to be included in the Comprehensive Plan. Councilmember Batos noted that he expected to meet with them, along with the consultants, in the next week or two. Ben Smith, LaRue Planning, Recreation and Open Space PowerPoint Presentation: Major Objectives - Master Planning; Interconnections; Coordination with State and Local Departments; Issues for Discussion and Decision. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Batos noted that he would like to speak with the consultants regarding the trail; limiting the trail on the rail line was not the direction proposed; there are other possibilities and there should be a broader area of opportunities that are being looked at by the MPO and the Village, and he would like that incorporated in the document. Councilmember Levitan agreed and believed that all of the elements needed to be coordinated. He spoke to the importance of entering into an agreement with an expert for a parks and recreation master plan, to ensure inclusion of all the levels of service, objectives and needs that should be required in Estero, which would be utilized in the Capital Improvements Plan and to determine the priorities. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November I, 2017 Page 7 of8 Public Comment: Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe, Chair of Coalition and Advisor to Estero High School Alumni Association, provided suggestions: sustainable identity of the Village throughout the Comprehensive Plan; distinction between passive and active reusable space; and Estero High School being included or recognized in the document as a signature recreational facility. Patty Whitehead, Old Estero, addressed concern with Council comments regarding CSX Rail corridor and other open space opportunities for preservation. Don Eslick, ECCL, addressed the westerly entrance to the park and development of 10 acres of the park. A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 13. ADJOURNMENT: 12:35 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: fa:tJu1,.,. Af4,ll,_; Kathy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November I, 2017 Page 8 of8

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2016-11-02
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village "Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL NOVEMBER 16, 2016 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting November 2, 2016 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Mark Goodman from Christ Community Ministries Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 ( excused absence) Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Pace, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Development Review Manager Walter McCarthy. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda as amended: changing agenda item 5 from Public Hearings to the topic of Coconut Road, renumbering agenda items 8 through 12, moving Workshop Items after Village Manager's Report. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 1 oflO Action: Approved the agenda as amended: changing agenda item 5 from Public Hearings to the topic of Coconut Road, renumbering agenda items 8 through 12, moving Workshop Items after Village Manager's Report. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 3. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval of October 19, 2016 Council Meeting Minutes Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 4. ORDINANCES: (a) FIRST READING: (1) Ordinance No. 2016-13 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Approving an Amendment to the Shoppes of Coconut Trace Commercial Planned Development Tract B, A1.9 Acre Property Located at 22942 Lyden Lane, to add Limited Commercial Uses with Conditions to Previously Approved Uses on Tract B of the Commercial Planned Development, to Approve a Deviation for a Sign Setback, and to Deny a Parking Reduction Deviation; Providing for Conflicts; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2016-13. Community Development Director Gibbs, provided a brief overview of the hearing to be held on December 7. Motion: Move to pass first reading of Ordinance No. 2016-13 and schedule second reading and public hearing for December 7, 2016. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 2 of IO Action: Passed first reading of Ordinance No. 2016-13 and scheduled second reading and public hearing for December 7, 2016. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 5. Coconut Road Village Manager Sarkozy and Community Development Director Gibbs provided explanations regarding a City of Bonita Springs Council workshop being held today regarding a presentation on the history and issues associated with the topic of whether to vacate the "Viewers Road" in the north western part of Bonita Springs. Discussion ensued. Public Comment: Persons speaking in favor of Village Council supporting Coconut Road to be maintained as a public road: Don Eslick Gene Cuomo Allen Hergenhahn BenKorbly Jerry Masters Ernie Parks Motion: Move to encourage Bonita Springs City Council to maintain Coconut Road as a public road, to authorize Village of Estero staff to appear at the Bonita Springs City Council Workshop on November 2, 2016 at 4:00 p.m. to voice the opinion of the Village Council, and to send an email to Carl Schwing and the Bonita Springs City Council regarding the position of the Village Council on this matter. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch and Errington. Brief discussion regarding working together with the Bonita Springs City Council on this important issue. Action: Approved encouraging Bonita Springs City Council to maintain Coconut Vote: Road as a public road, to authorize Village of Estero staff to appear at the Bonita Springs City Council Workshop on November 2, 2016 at 4:00 p.m. to voice the opinion of the Village Council, and to send an email to Carl Schwing and the Bonita Springs City Council regarding the position of the Village Council on this matter. Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 3 oflO 6. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Resolution No. 2016-33 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, FL, Establishing Meeting Dates, Time, and Location for Meetings of the Village ofEstero Council for 2017; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-33. Village Manager Sarkozy provided a briefreview of the information that was discussed at the October 19, 2016 Council workshop. Questions or Comments: Mayor Batos, Councilmember Wilson and Vice Mayor Levitan. Discussion regarding zoning hearing dates, which the Community Development Director would call to meet her schedule. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-33. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Wilson Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-33. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2016-34 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, FL, Calling for an Election for Village of Estero Council Members; Setting the Date; Providing for Ballot Language; Requesting the Lee County Supervisor of Elections to Conduct the Election and Certify the Results to the Estero Village Council; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2016-34. Village Attorney Saunders provided a summary and indicated that the resolution followed the Charter. He also noted a minor typographical error on page 2, which had been corrected in the original version of the document. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Ribble, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included other cities in Lee County that would be holding an election in March 2017; ballot language; and early voting not addressed in the resolution. Public Comment: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 4 oflO Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2016-34. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Councilmember Ribble Action: Adopted Resolution No. 2016-34. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (c) Takeover of Roads and Assumption of Road Maintenance Responsibilities from Lee County Village Manager Sarkozy and Assistant to Village Manager Coleman presented information regarding background: low taxes and staff levels; 2015-2016 and 2016-2017 agreements; map and listing of roads to be assumed; minor versus major maintenance; minor and major maintenance components with cost estimates; annual maintenance costs combined; and control/liability. Discussion also included the difficulty in translating County and comparable City costs to Village costs; uncertainty concerning contractor's ability to expand into Estero; the breadth of responsibilities with many tasks to assume, account for and the cost for; continuing the agreement for Natural Resources and Animal Services with Lee County; and providing 65-day notice to terminate Transportation portion of the agreement with Lee County. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Wilson, Ribble, Boesch, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included lane mile concept; non-political paving schedule; element of control; general fund dollars; addressing Estero Parkway as soon as possible; road light study on Estero Parkway which would be part of the Capital Improvement Program; contractors' insurance; concern regarding transfer of roads and FDOT approval; the county's collaboration throughout the process; concern regarding Corkscrew Road west of US 41; the roundabout at the southern terminus of Via Coconut; and contract language. Village Manager Sarkozy clarified that the Interlocal Agreement would come back to Council at the first meeting in December. Assistant to Village Manager Coleman explained the contract piggy-backing process that would begin in January. Brief discussion followed. Village Attorney Saunders indicated that it would be best for Council to approve all contracts; approvals could be done retroactively in order to allow the Village Manager to move things along. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to instruct staff to proceed with the process of preparing the documents to take over the roads and maintenance responsibilities from Lee County. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 5 oflO Action: Instructed staff to proceed with the process of preparing the documents to take over the roads and maintenance responsibilities from Lee County. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: Motion: Move to authorize the Village Manager to provide 65-day notice to Lee County for Village assumption of road maintenance responsibilities. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Authorized the Village Manager to provide 65-day notice to Lee County for Village assumption of road maintenance responsibilities. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: ( d) Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council Membership and Participation in Agency on Bay Management Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction. Tim Walker, GIS Manager, Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council, presented a video of the Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council annual report. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Ribble, Boesch, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included the free membership that was offered for the first year; coordinating a program for a path along the railroad; concern regarding changes in responsibilities for the Regional Planning Council (RPC) that have occurred over the years; and the Council request for the Executive Director or other staff to provide information regarding what the RPC does and the reasons why the Village should JOlil. Councilmember Errington announced that she resigned from the Estero Bay Agency on Bay Management two weeks ago; she enjoyed participating; however, as an elected official, she became the sounding board for folks that did not like decisions made by the Village Council. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to table the agenda item and extend another invitation to the Executive Director to come to a Council meeting to provide further information. Motion by: Seconded by: Vice Mayor Levitan Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 6 oflO Action: Tabled the agenda item and another invitation will be extended to the Executive Director to come to a Council meeting to provide further information. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (e) Salary Adjustment for Staff Village Manager Sarkozy explained the request for a cost of living increase, retroactive to October 1, 2016, which had been discussed as part of the budget process and included in the 2016-2017 fiscal year budget. Questions or Comments: Vice Mayor Levitan, Councilmembers Errington, Wilson, Ribble, Boesch, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included whether this could have been approved by the Village Manager; transparency; and Village staff pay scale. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve 4% increase for staff during fiscal year 2015-2016, including retroactive to October 1, 2016, for 4 staff members. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Ribble Action: Approved 4% increase for staff during fiscal year 2015-2016, including retroactive to October 1, 2016, for 4 staff members. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: (f) Health Care Coverage Village Manager Sarkozy provided an explanation regarding a proposed modification of the Village benefits policy that would add spousal or dependent child coverage, with the affected employee paying 25% of the total monthly cost through payroll deduction. He noted that all other local cities and the County offered dependent health care. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve a modification to the Benefits Policy that would add spousal or dependent child coverage, with the affected employee paying 25% of the cost. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 7 oflO Action: Approved a modification to the Benefits Policy that would add spousal or dependent child coverage, with the affected employee paying 25% of the cost. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call; Councilmember Brown absent) Nay: Abstentions: 7. UNFINISHED BUSINESS: None. 8. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Appearance by: Gary Israel, Stoneybrook, addressed concern regarding truck traffic 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble: noted that Giordano & Associates had not provided Council with their performance reassessment that was discussed in August. He also suggested that Council take a look at their contract in helping with some of the responsibilities involved with public works. Councilmember Errington: addressed concern regarding apartments for FGCU student housing and the number of additional projects. She received a call from a concerned citizen that the student housing was inundated with parties. She suggested that Council look at all areas of the Village and decide how Council wanted the areas to look. Councilmember Boesch: addressed the sand re-nourishment program for Collier county; trucks will be utilizing Alico Road; administrators from the Lee County Sheriffs Office offered help. He suggested that Council request additional surveillance as well as truck inspections. Brief discussion ensued. Councilmember Wilson: suggested working together with Grandezza for next year's community-wide Heart Walk event. Mayor Batos: provided a list of requested Council workshops, asked that the Village Manager look at possible items for workshops and encouraged Councilmembers to do the same. 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. 11. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. Adjourned Regular Session at 11:42 a.m. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 8 oflO Convened Workshop Items at 11:53 a.m. 12. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Estero Fine Arts Show - Patty Narozny, Director Village Manager Sarkozy introduced the Director of the Estero Fine Arts Show. Patty Narozny, Director, Estero Fine Arts Show, presented information regarding the 18th bi-annual event, indicated this was one of Estero's top cultural events, and noted that the important component was the youth art competition for ages 5 - 12 or grades K - 8. Questions or Comments by: Councilmember Boesch inquired regarding the time of the event and Vice Mayor Levitan inquired whether they had been issued a permit for the event. (b) US 41 Lighting Project Update Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief introduction. Jeff Mednick, Design Consultant Project Management, Florida Department of Transportation, presented information regarding the scope of the project; LED highway lighting; project schedule; and construction efforts, including light poles, underground electrical and lane closures. Questions or Comments: Councilmernber Boesch, Vice Mayor Levitan and Mayor Batos. Discussion included light pole mounting brackets; ordinance and approval process for banner hanging. (c) Council Rules of Procedure Village Manager Sarkozy introduced the agenda item and reviewed that, previously, there had been a suggestion to enhance the rules with job descriptions for Mayor, Vice Mayor and Councilmembers. Village Attorney Saunders stated that the draft rules were distributed to the Council and comments were subsequently provided by Vice Mayor Levitan, which were also distributed to the Council. Discussion followed related to the Vice Mayor's proposed amendments. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Boesch requested that a rule be added to allow for input on a motion at a meeting and bringing the motion up at a subsequent meeting for discussion and consideration. Mayor Batos requested that a rule be added to allow for a Councilmember to vote on motions while participating in a Council meeting via teleconference equipment. Councilmember Errington reported that she would be attending a Veterans Day event at the Elks Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 9 oflO Club and would be reading and presenting a proclamation. She requested that Council hold a combined workshop with the Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board. She also requested that Council explore the legality of utilizing drones. Village Manager Sarkozy alerted Council to two items in the desk packet: (1) Letter regarding Truth in Millage (TRIM) Certification from Dr. Maurice M. Gogarty, Director, Property Tax Oversight Program; (2) Event information from Estero Bay Buddies regarding a dinner and silent auction - Estero Bay Aquatic Preserve 50th Anniversary, December 2, 5:00 p.m. until 10:00 p.m., Hyatt Regency Coconut Point Resort and Spa. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 13. ADJOURNMENT: 1:08 p.m. ATTEST: By: . /p.tqu#.J.J/J.Lt Katliy HalC MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - November 2, 2016 Page 10 oflO

title: Special Meeting Minutes - 2015-11-13
raw_text: VILLAGE COUNCIL SPECIAL MEETING OF NOVEMBER 13, 2015 The Village of Estero Council Special Meeting was held on this date at the Estero Fire Rescue District Meeting Room, 21500 Three Oaks Parkway, Estero, Florida. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice-Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 10:00 a.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE Mayor Batos led the Pledge of Allegiance. 3. ROLLCALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present. 4. APPROVAL OF AGENDA Councilmember Ribble moved approval of the agenda, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously. 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-17 An Ordinance of the Village ofEstero, Florida, Providing for an Additional Homestead Exemption of Fifty Thousand Dollars ($50,000) for Persons Sixty-Five (65) and Older with Certain Income Limits; Providing an Additional Homestead Exemption Equal to the Assessed Value of the Property with a Just Value Less than Two Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($250,000) Where the Owner has Maintained Thereon Permanent Residence for at least Twenty Five (25) Years, and has Attained Age Sixty-Five (65) with Certain Income Limits; Providing for Authority; Providing for Short Title; Providing for Definitions; Providing for Criteria for Additional Exemptions; Providing for Application Procedure; Providing for Property Held Jointly with Right of Survivorship; Providing for Annual Adjustment of the Income Limitation; Providing for Conflicting Provisions and Severability; Providing for Filing with the Department of State and Lee County Property Appraiser; Providing for Codification and Scrivener's Errors; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Ordinance No. 2015-17 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - November 13, 2015 Page 1 of3 Village Attorney Saunders explained that the exemption was previously in place through Lee County and indicated that the total maximum exposure in terms oflost tax revenue was approximately $15,000 to $20,000. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Mayor Batos noted that, in the future, Council would need information regarding financial ramifications for any agenda item with a financial impact. Village Manager Sarkozy acknowledged the request and noted that his standard approach would be to bring to Council the short and long term fiscal impact on any agenda item. Councilmember Boesch moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-17, seconded by Councilmember Wilson, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (B) Resolution No. 2015-74 A Resolution of the Village ofEstero, Florida, Approving the Tidewater Plat; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2015-74 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Community Development Director Gibbs provided an explanation of the resolution. Discussion ensued regarding the plat approval process and suggestions for possible rev1s1ons. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Vice Mayor Levitan disclosed ex parte communication related to the plat approval process directly with representatives from Pulte, including Chris Hasty. Councilmember Boesch moved to adopt Resolution No. 2015-74, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (C) Resolution No. 2015-75 A Resolution of the Village ofEstero, Florida, Repealing Resolution No. 2015-08 and Resolution No. 2015-16; Designating a Depository for Receipt of Funds and Payment of Debts; Directing the Mayor and Village Manager to Execute Checks; Authorizing the Vice Mayor to Act as a Signatory on Village Accounts in the Absence of the Mayor or Village Manager; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2015-75 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Finance Director Pace provided an explanation of the resolution. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Wilson moved to adopt Resolution No. 2015-75, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - November 13, 2015 Page 2 of3 6. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Mayor Batos called for public input on any issue and no one came forward. 7. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS None. 8. VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS None. 9. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS None. 10. ADJOURN Councilmember Errington moved to adjourn, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 10:17 a.m. ATTEST: Kathy Hall, MMC Village Clerk Minutes approved as presented: December 16, 2015 Village Council Special Meeting Minutes - November 13, 2015 Nicholas Batos Mayor Page 3 of3 VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP OF NOVEMER 13, 2015 The Village of Estero Council Workshop was held on this date at the Estero Fire Rescue District Meeting Room, 21500 Three Oaks Parkway, Estero, Florida. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice-Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Peter Lombardi, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 10:18 a.m. 2. ROLLCALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present. 3. APPROVAL OF AGENDA Mayor Batos removed agenda item 3 (B). Councilmember Ribble moved approval of the agenda as amended, seconded by Councilmember Brown, called and carried unanimously. 4. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) Presentation: Horizon Council Foundation (Councilmember Wilson) Glen Salyer, Economic Development Director of Lee County and Assistant to County Manager, through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding economic development partnership; Horizon Council; strategic support; commercial corridors; task forces; Horizon Foundation; industrial development authority; economic development office; business development; partnership successes; and success stories. Council questions and discussion followed related to junior achievement and companies providing help to students in obtaining meaningful part time jobs; economic opportunities; private sector and non-profit contributions to the Horizon Foundation; tourism; Horizon Council membership; and sunshine laws. Public Comment: Jim Shields, Grandezza resident, spoke regarding junior achievement. Village Council Workshop Minutes - November 13, 2015 Page 1 of4 (B) Economic Development (Mayor Batos)-Agenda item removed by Mayor Batos. Note: Agenda item 4 (D) was addressed prior to agenda item 4 (C). (C) Fire Rescue Interlocal Agreements (Vice Mayor Levitan) Vice Mayor Levitan provided background information regarding the resolutions that were tabled at the July 1, 2015 Council meeting and explained the amendments made to the Interlocal Agreement with Estero Fire Rescue District, noting that there would be amendments to the Interlocal Agreement with San Carlos Fire Rescue District as well. He also noted that there may be a need to adopt an Interlocal Agreement with Bonita Springs Fire Control and Rescue District. Council questions and discussion ensued and Vice Mayor Levitan provided detailed explanation regarding concurrent jurisdiction on impact fees; providing collaboration on rates; and setting an agreement term at 10 years. Scott Vanderbrook, Chief, Estero Fire Rescue District, stated that setting a 10-year term was acceptable and noted that collection of impact fees would be complicated. David Cambareri, Chief, San Carlos Fire District, stated that he was aware of the proposed Interlocal Agreements and that he and ChiefVanderbrook had been working together. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. (D) Potential Northern Section 951 (Mayor Batos) Mayor Batos provided a brief introduction. Don Scott, Executive Director, Lee County Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO), through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding the status of the CR 951 extension. Council questions and discussion followed. Public Comment: Jim Shields, Grandezza resident, addressed concern regarding home values as long as the extension remained on the long range plan; it should be removed from the plan. Jim Varey, Grandezza resident, representing East Corkscrew Coalition and on behalf of Grandezza residents, addressed concern regarding traffic and decline in property values. Don Eslick, ECCL, provided background information and requested that Council adopt a resolution requesting Lee County MPO remove the portion of proposed CR 951 extension from the 2040 needs plan. Bill Williams, Vice Chairman, MPO Citizens Advisory Council (CAC), echoed other speakers' comments, requested Council to adopt a resolution to remove the proposed CR 951 extension from the 2040 needs plan, and stated that he would make that action at the December CAC meeting. Village Council Workshop Minutes - November 13, 2015 Page 2 of4 Council discussion ensued regarding taking action at the November 18, 2015 Council meeting. (D) Solid Waste Franchise Fee Agreement Update Village Attorney Saunders provided information related to the issues with the agreement, including term, how the payout would be made to the Village, and removal of debris during a disaster. Council discussion followed. Public Comment: David Cambareri, Chief, San Carlos Fire District, stated that on the emergency management side with disaster cleanup, the county was divided into geographical divisions and they assist with the process. He indicated that there should be no concern that there would be a delay in the cleanup process. Bill Williams stated that he had experience with these types of agreements, most were 20 year terms, and he supported the Council's efforts. Village Attorney Saunders indicated that he had sufficient information from Council and would reach out to Lee County. 5. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Don Eslick, ECCL, provided a report of activities over the last 8 months and spoke to the role of ECCL. Beverly MacNellis, Marsh Landing resident, provided a reminder regarding the "Light up Estero" event on December 5, 2015 and announced the gala grand opening and showing of the premier film of Mount Key on February 20, 2016. 6. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS I FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS Councilmember Ribble provided information related to the final drawings of the interior work for 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle and proposals for video recording meetings. He also spoke regarding a scope of services from Joe Minicozzi related to a process resolving some of the land use options in the Village Center. Discussion ensued regarding the proposal and whether to move forward. It was determined that the matter would be placed on the November 18, 2015 Council meeting agenda. Vice Mayor Levitan spoke regarding meetings with representatives of the new hospital and internal development on a 38 acre site. Councilmember Errington inquired regarding removal of specific items from the agenda. Detailed discussion followed related to the agenda process and meetings schedule. Councilmember Wilson offered compliments to the ECCL and stated that he supported the ECCL role concept. Village Council Workshop Minutes - November 13, 2015 Page 3 of 4 Mayor Batos stated that he believed the Council had accomplished a great deal in the past 8 months. 7. VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS None. 8. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS None. 9. ADJOURN A motion to adjourn was duly made, called and carried unanimously. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 12:46 p.m. ATTEST: By: J(~ t.JI..L<. i Kathy Hall, MMC Village Clerk Minutes approved as submitted: December 16, 2015 Village Council Workshop Minutes - November 13, 2015 Nicholas Batos Mayor Page 4 of 4
