title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2016-12-07
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-f1.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL JANUARY 4, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting December 7, 2016 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:30 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Todd Weston from River of Life Assembly of God. Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 (left the meeting at 12:30 p.m.), Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Village Clerk Kathy Hall, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Community Development Director Mary Gibbs. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes- December 7, 2016 Page 1 of9 3. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Approval ofNovember 16, 2016 Council Meeting Minutes Vice Mayor Levitan noted a typographical error on page 1 and amended the word "manipulating" to "revising" on page 6, paragraph 10. Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 4. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Stormwater Master Plan Consultant Selection (CN 2016-02) Village Manager Sarkozy noted that the Stormwater Master Plan would be one of the major processes for the Village occurring in 2017. Community Development Director Gibbs provided details regarding the consultant interview and selection process from the eight submittals received; final ranking was JR Evans Engineering number one, Tetra Tech number two and Johnson Engineering number three; the contract will be reviewed by Council at a later date. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Boesch, Errington, and Vice Mayor Levitan. Discussion included budgeted funds; the process for reaching an agreement; Edison Farms; and water flow. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to concur with the Competitive Negotiations staff review committee recommendation of award for CN 2016-02 and authorize the Village Manager to negotiate an agreement with the number one ranked firm of J.R. Evans Engineering to prepare the Village's Stormwater Master Plan. Motion by: Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Councilmember Boesch Action: Concurred with the Competitive Negotiations staff review committee recommendation of award for CN 2016-02 and authorized the Village Manager to negotiate an agreement with the number one ranked firm of J .R. Evans Engineering to prepare the Village's Stormwater Master Plan. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - December 7, 2016 Page 2 of9 Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: (b) Interlocal Agreement By and Between Lee County and the Village ofEstero - Ownership, Jurisdiction, Operation, and Maintenance of Certain Roads Village Manager Sarkozy stated that this was the first area where the Village will have a major day-to-day operation in the field and that it was a natural step in the evolution of the Village. He indicated that the $2.6M that has been provided to Lee County for road maintenance, plus any available Road Impact Fee resources, was thought to be adequate to maintain the Village road system in top condition. Village Attorney Saunders addressed a revision on page 6 of the interlocal agreement that was provided to Council. Questions or Comments by: Councilmembers Ribble, Wilson, Boesch, Errington, Vice Mayor Levitan, and Mayor Batos. Discussion included the issue of the county's denial that Corkscrew Road west of US 41 was a county road, although a research memorandum had been provided. The Village Manager suggested caution regarding maintenance responsibilities for that section of Corkscrew Road. Discussion also included the references in the agreement regarding canals and the possible consequences of delaying approval of the agreement until the January 4, 2017 Council meeting. It was decided to hold a meeting on December 20, 2016 for this agenda item. title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2015-12-11
raw_text: ont VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP OF DECEMBER 11, 2015 The Village of Estero Council Workshop was held on this date at the Estero Fire Rescue District Meeting Room, 21500 Three Oaks Parkway, Estero, Florida. Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Absent: Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3 (excused absence). Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 1. CALL TO ORDER Mayor Batos called the meeting to order at 10:00 a.m. 2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE Mayor Batos led the Pledge of Allegiance. 3. ROLL CALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present with the exception of Councilmember Brown (excused absence). 4. APPROVAL OF AGENDA Vice Mayor Levitan moved approval of the agenda, seconded by Councilmember Boesch, called and carried unanimously, with Councilmember Brown absent. 5. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) _ Presentation: Lee County Sheriff's Office - Captain Keith Day and Lieutenant Dennis Petracca Captain Day provided information regarding reports for the Village of Estero, with concentration on traffic issues. Discussion included traffic; crimes reported for the month of November; homeless camps; and an issue in Pelican Landing that was handled successfully. Lieutenant Petracca provided information regarding accumulation of speed data for Estero; in general, he believed that there wasn'ta huge speed issue. Discussion involved commercial trucks; traffic surveys during "season"; citizen complaints in Bella Terra; and the possibility of having a presence on an ongoing basis. Discussion followed regarding utilizing existing resources, allowing time for further traffic study that would provide better information, and placement of the speed machines. Village Council Workshop Minutes - December 11, 2015 Page 1 of 4 (B) (C) (D) Public Comment: Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe resident, inquired regarding a full time deputy on staff to meet with the Homeowners Associations to address safety. Brief discussion followed regarding the ability of the Sheriff's office issuing tickets and warnings within gated communities. Don Eslick, ECCL, urged Council to place the reports on the website and addressed egress problems. Mayor Batos suggested that the reports include whether they believed that accidents were being caused by egress issues. Presentation: Lovers Key State Park - Rich Donnelly and Mark Generales Mark Generales explained that the Friends of Lovers Key were seeking endorsement, and requesting that the Council adopt a resolution and add the project to the list of legislative priorities, of an environmental education center which they were seeking funding assistance from the State. Through a PowerPoint presentation, Mr. Generales provided park information related to attendance, beach access, nature trails, revenues, volunteers, staff, and economic impact. (Written materials provided) Rich Donnelly, building designer, provided information regarding the 3600 sq. ft. LEED certified building. Discussion ensued regarding a resolution of the Council to be prepared for the next regular Council meeting. Public Comment: Patty Whitehead, Old Estero resident, inquired whether there would be a public comment period for the building design. Don Eslick, ECCL, spoke regarding the MPO 2040 Plan, and noted that the water element was the top priority. He also spoke regarding an extensive study that was done related to a shuttle boat system, which he encouraged the Council to look into. Zip Code Issue Update Village Attorney Saunders reported that contact was made with Curt Clawson's office and the response was that they were still working on it; a letter was also sent to the Director of the Postal Service. The meeting went into recess at 11:20 a.m. and reconvened at 11:26 a.m. Community Development - Outline Process for Upcoming Rezoning Hearings Community Development Director Mary Gibbs provided detailed information regarding the quasi-judicial process for the rezoning hearings that would begin in January. Council questions and discussion followed. Village Council Workshop Minutes - December 11, 2015 Page 2 of 4 Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. (E) Council Meeting Procedures and Protocols (Discussion of draft) Village Attorney Saunders spoke to the draft Rules of Procedure and discussion followed. The Village Manager proposed that this be a workshop item on January 20, 2016 and Councilmembers were encouraged to provide comments to him prior to that time. Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. 8. PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Bill Williams, representing Lee County Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO) Citizens Advisory Committee (CAC), provided reports regarding the November and December 2015 CAC meetings, noting that the CAC voted to remove the CR 951 extension project from the 2040 Long Range Transportation Plan/Needs List at the November meeting. He indicated that an affirmative vote of the MPO on December 18, 2015 would make it official. (Written materials provided) Ed Weil, Rookery Pointe resident and Estero High School girls' varsity soccer team coach, announced that the team had won 14 straight wins and a fundraising event would take place at Culver's next Monday. 9. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS Councilmember Errington inquired regarding Regional Planning Council. Mayor Batos requested that the issue be placed on the next regular meeting agenda to provide formal declaration regarding membership. Councilmember Boesch spoke to an article related to water in the News Press. Councilmember Wilson noted the importance of obtaining further traffic volume information from the Sheriff's office prior to the Village going forward with the traffic study. Mayor Batos stated that the MPO would be contacting the Village Manager regarding information on how the Village should put a request for proposal out for the traffic study, which should be done during "season." , 10. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS None. 11, VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS None. Village Council Workshop Minutes - December 11, 2015 Page 3 of 4 12. ADJOURN Councilmember Ribble moved to adjourn, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried unanimously, with Councilmember Brown absent. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 12:08 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF E RO, FLORIDA By: Fritthig Mat By: Kathy Hall, MMC Nicholas Batos Village Clerk Mayor Minutes approved as submitted: January 6, 2016 Village Council Workshop Minutes ~ December 11, 2015 Page 4 of 4

PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE Mayor Batos led the Pledge of Allegiance. 3. INVOCATION Father Tony from Our Lady of Light Catholic Community offered the invocation. 4. ROLL CALL Village Clerk Kathy Hall called the roll; all members were present with the exception of Councilmember Brown (excused absence). 5. APPROVAL OF AGENDA Councilmember Errington stated that she wanted to remove agenda item 8 (C) First reading of Ordinance No. 2015-21 from the agenda and postpone first reading until Council was provided the related materials. Discussion ensued, including the quasi-judicial nature of the hearing, and Village Attorney Saunders stated that all documentation would be distributed well in advance of the public hearing and second reading. Community Development Department Director Gibbs provided Florida statute information, stated that she would be willing to brief each Councilmember when the related materials became available, and noted that if there wasn'ta clear understanding at the hearing, it could be continued. Councilmember Ribble moved approval of the agenda, seconded by Councilmember Wilson, called and carried unanimously, with Councilmember Brown absent. Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes - December 16, 2015 Page 1 of 6 6. CONSENT AGENDA (A) - Approval of Minutes: (1) November 13, 2015 Special Council Meeting (2) November 13, 2015 Council Workshop Councilmember Levitan moved approval of the Consent Agenda, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. 7. PRESENTATION Jeffrey Mednick, Project Manager, Florida Department of Transportation - US 41 Lighting Project Jeff Mednick, Project Manager, through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding the US 41 lighting project South of Pelican Colony Blvd to San Carlos Blvd; project history; scope of project; budget and schedule; FDOT coordination with entities; commitments made in 2012; LED highway lighting; contractor's selection process; decorative options requested in 2012 by ECCL; decorative options for now and for the future; Village concurrence and maintenance agreement. Mr. Mednick also spoke briefly regarding the template maintenance agreement and template compensation agreement. (Written materials provided) Discussion followed related to federal funding; the type of light that would be utilized if the Council chose to move forward at this time; there was no guarantee when this project would be funded again if the Council chose decorative lighting options and not the standard LED lights; maintenance and compensation; banner fixtures and the need for a program, including the banner approval process through FDOT. There was Council consensus to move forward and schedule approval for the next regular Council meeting, January 6, 2016. 8. COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) Second Reading and Public Hearing: Ordinance No. 2015-20 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Amending the Land Development Code, Chapter 2, Article VI, Impact Fees, Division 2, Roads Impact Fees, Section 2-266 Revising the Roads Impact Fee Schedule to Update the Calculations of the Full Costs; Providing for Conflict; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2015-20 and noted that the notice was published pursuant to Florida statutes, on November 27, 2015. Community Development Director Gibbs provided explanation of the ordinance and the study. Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes -- December 16, 2015 Page 2 of 6 () Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Boesch moved to adopt Ordinance No. 2015-20, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. Second Reading and Public Hearing: Ordinance No. 2015-19 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Amending Chapter 34, Zoning, Article VII, Supplementary District Regulations, Division 15, Estero Land Development Code, to Limit Certain Excavation Activities Relating to Well Stimulation Including Acid Stimulation and Hydraulic or Acid Fracturing; Providing for Codification; Providing for Conflict with Other Ordinances; and Providing for an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2015-19 and noted that the public hearing notices were published pursuant to Florida statutes, on November 25, 2015 and December 11, 2015. Mayor Batos opened the hearing. Public testimony presented in favor of the ordinance: Dr. John Dwyer, Naples resident Dr. Karen Dwyer, Naples resident Phil Douglas, Lighthouse Bay resident (Written testimony provided) Peter Cangialosi, The Preserve at Corkscrew resident, representing ECCL (Written testimony provided) Ray Judah, Iona McGregor community resident, representing FCOC Beverly MacNellis, Marsh Landing resident Caitlin Weber, Conservancy of Southwest Florida (Written testimony provided) Julianne Thomas, Conservancy of Southwest Florida Ralf Brookes, Attorney, Conservancy of Southwest Florida Jaimie Duran, Naples resident Donna Knapp, Naples resident Ruth McGregor, No. Fort Myers resident The public hearing was closed. Village Attorney Saunders called attention to revisions made to the ordinance following the first reading. Councilmember Exrington moved to adopt Ordinance No. 2015-19 as amended, seconded by Councilmember Boesch. Discussion of the motion followed. Vice Mayor Levitan noted that Council was provided over 1,000 pages of documents relating to fracking; he focused on two in particular, the June 2015 report from New York State Department of Environmental Conservation, and the June 2015 Assessment of the Potential Impacts of Hydraulic Fracturing for Oil and Gas on Drinking Water Resources Executive Summary published by the Federal Environment Protection Agency. Vice Mayor Levitan also noted for the record that no one appeared before the Council that was against the ordinance, the Planning and Zoning Board found conclusively that the ordinance was consistent with the Comprehensive Plan, and voted to strongly recommend that Council Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes - December 16, 2015 Page 3 of 6 (C) adopt the ordinance. He also referred to the preamble to the Estero Comprehensive Plan as well as Goal 19 Vision Statement. He stated that he believed that the ordinance was entirely consistent with the Comprehensive Plan and that the only way to protect quality of life in Estero was to ban the practice completely. Councilmember Errington conveyed appreciation to the Conservancy for all of the work involved and stated that water was the important issue. She agreed with Vice Mayor Levitan and noted that she has not had one person ask her to support fracking. Councilmember Boesch commented that the emphasis was on water, especially in this area. He suggested that the H2O Solutions report be included in the record. Attorney Saunders responded that all documents presented at the meeting would be part of the record, Mayor Batos stated that he was told that if the Council passed the ordinance and the Bill passed as written, the ordinance would be preempted; however, if the wording in the Bill was rewritten, the portion that would preempt the Village from controlling the zoning would be reinstated. He continued that he was not in favor of fracking; however, he wanted to be practical and put in laws that meant something. He stated that his responsibility was to represent the people; although he did not believe that the ordinance would accomplish anything, he would be in favor of the ordinance because he sensed that was what most people wanted. The motion was called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. The meeting went into recess at 7:55 p.m. and reconvened at 8:02 p.m. First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-21 A Zoning Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida; Rezoning 21.36 Acres Located at 9201 and 9301 Corkscrew Road, from Commercial Planned Development to Residential Planned Development for the Volunteers of America Continuing Care/Assisted Living Project, with a Maximum 340 Beds and Maximum Building Height of 45 Feet, and Including Conditions of Development; Approving Certain Deviations; Providing for Conflict; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance 2015-21and noted that the zoning hearing would be held on January 13, 2015 and the public hearing notice was published pursuant to Florida statutes, on December 11, 2015. Community Development Director Gibbs provided a brief explanation of the ordinance as well as information related to the zoning hearing process. Note: Public comment could not be taken on Ordinance No. 2015-21 until the second reading and public hearing scheduled for January 13, 2016. Mayor Batos asked that the applicant provide electronic copies of information. Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes - December 16, 2015 Page 4 of 6 Councilmember Boesch moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-21, seconded by Councilmember Wilson, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. (D) Resolution No. 2015-81 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Supporting the Budget Request for the Lovers Key Environmental Educational Center; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Resolution No. 2015-81. Vice Mayor Levitan noted a revision that should be made to Section 2 to indicate that Council supported the funding request to the state legislature. Section 2 was amended to read: "The Village Council of the Village of Estero supports the funding request to the state legislature made by Friends of Lovers Key in the amount of $1.5 million for Phase 1 of the Environmental Education Center." Mayor Batos called for public comment and no one came forward. Councilmember Ribble moved to adopt Resolution No. 2015-81 as amended, seconded by Vice Mayor Levitan, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (E) Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council Membership Mayor Batos explained that Councilmember Errington brought this up at the December 11, 2015 Council workshop. Councilmember Errington stated that she confirmed that she could continue as the liaison to the Estero Bay Agency on Bay Management. Councilmember Errington moved to authorize the Mayor to sign the letter as well as being able to make technical changes to the letter if necessary, seconded by Councilmember Wilson. Discussion of the motion followed related to the future of the Regional Planning Council. The motion was called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. 9, PUBLIC INPUT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS Mayor Batos called for public input on any issue and no one came forward. 10. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS Councilmember Ribble reviewed the history and provided an update related to the building located at 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle. Discussion ensued regarding additional funds necessary for occupancy, including furniture, fixtures, and equipment. There was consensus to bring back a budget amendment to the next regular Council meeting. Councilmember Errington extended appreciation to Bill Smith for the nice Estero River article that was in the News Press. Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes - December 16, 2015 Page 5 of 6 11. VILLAGE ATTORNEY COMMENTS (A) Suggested Legislative Priorities for the 2016 Legislative Session Village Attorney Saunders provided a brief explanation of his proposed 2016 State Legislative Agenda, which included 9 Bills. He noted that he would be adding one additional Bill related to municipal election dates. He indicated that he would like to begin working with staff on projects that they would like to get funded. Brief discussion followed. Mayor Batos inquired whether a letter was sent to the Capitol in support of the Governor's funding attempt for bringing business into the state. Village Attorney Saunders responded that he would need to research whether a letter was sent. Mayor Batos stated that, if a letter of support was sent to the Governor, it should be added to the priority list. 12, VILLAGE MANAGER COMMENTS None. 13. ADJOURN Councilmember Errington moved to adjourn, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously, with Councilmember Brown absent. Mayor Batos adjourned the meeting at 8:25 p.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: -Hethiy Sale By: -FZ =a Kathy Hall, MMC Nicholas Batos Village Clerk Mayor Minutes approved as submitted: January 6, 2016 Village Council Regular Meeting Minutes - December 16, 2015 Page 6 of 6

title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2016-12-20
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. APPROVED BY COUNCIL JANUARY 18, 2017 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 Village Council Meeting December 20, 2016 9:30 a.m. 1. OPENING EXERCISES: (Includes call to order, roll call, pledge of allegiance, announcements, proclamations, and ceremonial presentations) Call to Order: 9:31 a.m. Pledge of Allegiance: Led by Mayor Batos. Invocation: Offered by Pastor Doug Warren from Gulf Coast Presbyterian Church. Roll Call: Present: Mayor Nick Batos - District 6, Vice Mayor Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Bill Ribble, District 1, Councilmember Donald Brown - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, and Executive Assistant to Village Manager Carol Sacco. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Brown Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - December 20, 2016 Page 1 of4 3. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Interlocal Agreement By and Between Lee County and the Village of Estero - Ownership, Jurisdiction, Operation, and Maintenance of Certain Roads Village Manager Sarkozy provided information regarding minor modifications made to the agreement and spoke to the Village's responsibility related to ditches. He also reviewed the county's denial that Corkscrew Road west of US 41 was a county- maintained road; for purposes of this document, the county has conveyed to the Village all of their rights, responsibilities, and obligations, as well as a quit claim deed. He added that, under Florida State statue, there are a number of roads within each jurisdiction where ownership of a road is in question and the Village would be dealing with that for some time. He noted that Village Attorneys Burt Saunders and Derek Rooney negotiated final language of the agreement. Councilmember Wilson inquired whether only surface water management and animal services would remain intact at the level previously agreed to. Village Manager Sarkozy responded that was correct. Mayor Batos conveyed appreciation to Councilmembers Ribble and Boesch, as well as Village Manager Sarkozy, for all of the work involved with the agreement. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to approve the Interlocal Agreement which formally transfers all responsibilities from the County to the Village effective January 7, 2017. Motion by: Councilmember Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved the Interlocal Agreement which formally transfers all responsibilities from the County to the Village effective January 7, 2017. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (roll call) Nay: Abstentions: 4. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. 5. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS/ FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Ribble: Provided a report on the Estero High School Culinary Program fundraiser that he attended on December 19, 2016. He also suggested role of Council liaison as a future workshop item. Vice Mayor Levitan: Commented on the end of year financial report and stated that he was amazed how the Village ran under "Government Lite." He also conveyed thanks to the volunteers on the Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - December 20, 2016 Page 2 of4 Councilmember Errington: Provided information regarding a recent seminar she attended at FGCU concerning rising sea level and suggested a Council workshop on the topic. She echoed Vice Mayor Levitan's comments and spoke regarding the Estero High School Culinary Program fundraiser. Councilmember Boesch: Commented regarding a report from Ed Weil related to parks and suggested a meeting with Mr. Weil and his group. He addressed concerns raised regarding possible conflicts of interest related to Village Attorney Saunders, due to recently being elected to the Collier County Board of Commissioners, and suggested that the Council discuss the matter. He offered positive comments regarding the great team effort in order to accomplish approval of the Interlocal Agreement. Councilmember Wilson: Shared that it was a pleasure to serve on a Council where one of the biggest challenges was building reserves and making important decisions on what the Village would do with the surplus, which was not common in his previous experience as a Councilmember elsewhere. He reported that he accepted the liaison position with Southwest Florida Regional Planning Council and would keep the Village Council up to date with issues that impact Estero. Mayor Batos: Discussed his memorandum related to capital improvements through possible stimulus grants, wayfinding sign plan, and SUNTrails, topics of discussion at the December meetings of the Metropolitan Planning Organization (MPO) which he attended. He noted that, in the past, the MPO looked at projects that were "shovel ready" and suggested that Council ask staff to prepare a list of projects to be considered for submittal to the MPO, in the event a stimulus plan became available. The items he suggested were: repaving ofEstero Parkway, sidewalk project on Estero Parkway, all repaving work recommended in the FGCU report, and any other roads that staff or Council could add. Regarding the wayfinding sign plan, Mayor Batos reported that the MPO was permitted to use money left over from a TIGER grant to have wayfinding signs placed around the county; Estero will be receiving some of them. The Council will be asked to pass a resolution allowing the placement of the signs and possibly agreeing to future sign maintenance. Representatives of the MPO will be attending a Council meeting early next year to make a presentation and ask for Council input and approval of a resolution. Mayor Batos noted that he received a call from Bonita Springs Mayor Peter Simmons requesting that both Councils collaborate on SUNTrails. Peter O'Flinn, City of Bonita Springs District 4 Councilmember, provided insight regarding SUNTrails and spoke to the shared strategies for the future, including protecting land, the DR/GR, higher design standards, inter-connectivity, and multi-modal bicycle trails. He stated that there have been large sums of money given for inter-coastal bike connectors and other elements. He believed that this would be a great thing for all to investigate collectively and thoroughly. He reiterated that the Bonita Springs City Council would like to meet with the Village of Estero Council. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - December 20, 2016 Page 3 of 4 Discussion ensued regarding endorsement of the concept of working with the Bonita Springs Council and scheduling a workshop. It was suggested that, as this moved forward, a Village Council liaison be appointed to work with a Bonita Springs Council liaison and report back to each Council, so that the responsibility did not fall on the Village's limited staff. There was Council consensus for the Village Manager to arrange a joint workshop for the topic of SUNTrails. 6. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. 7. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. A motion to adjourn was duly made and passed. 8. ADJOURNMENT: 10:03 a.m. ATTEST: VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: ,Qf<ar/vJ JJtLL,L-- Kathy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - December 20, 2016 Page 4 of4

APPROVED BY COUNCIL MARCH 6, 2019 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES VILLAGE COUNCIL MEETING Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 January 9,2019 9:30 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER: 9:30 a.m. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Mayor Boesch. INVOCATION: Pastor Todd Weston from River of Life Assembly of God. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Land Use Counsel Nancy Stroud, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the revised agenda. Motion by: Vice Mayor Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 1 of 8 3. PROCLAMATION: (a) Human Trafficking Awareness and Prevention Month A Proclamation was presented to Lorna McCraney, Program Director and Sam Lewis, CEO, WearTheFund, Human Trafficking Awareness Partnerships, proclaiming the month of January, 2019 as "Human Trafficking Awareness and Prevention Month." 4, PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None 5. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) First Reading of Ordinances: Ordinance No. 2019-02 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Licenses and Business Regulations Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Ordinance No. 2019-09 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Tourist Development Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 2 of 8 Ordinance No. 2019-10 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Water and Sewers Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Ordinance No. 2019-11 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Wells Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Ordinance No. 2019-12 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Non-Ordinance Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the Ordinances for the record. Village Attorney Saunders provided brief comments regarding the ordinances and referenced a memo from Attorney Derek Rooney. Public Comment: None Motion: Move to approve consent agenda Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Questions or Comments: None 6. CONSIDERATION OF ITEMS DEFERRED FROM CONSENT AGENDA: None 7. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Resolution No. 2019-02 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Authorizing the Village Manager to Record in the Lee County Records this Resolution and its Attachments, Including a Three Party Agreement Related to the Purchase of Property Currently Subject to the Estero on the River MPD Resolution Approved by Lee County Board of County Commissioners on June 4, 2007, as Zoning Resolution Z-07-021; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2019-02 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Village Attorney Saunders noted concerns from Councilmember Levitan and noted that Village Land Use Counsel Stroud came up with this resolution to solve the issue. Questions or Comments: None Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 3 of 8 Public Comment: None Motion: Move to adopt Resolution No. 2019-02. Motion by: Councilmember Errington Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: (b) Resolution No. 2019-01 A Resolution of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Authorizing the Issuance of Its Not To Exceed $20,000,000 Taxable Revenue Note, Series 2019, for the Purpose of Financing, In Part, the Cost of Purchasing Certain Parcels of Land, as More Fully Described Herein; Authorizing the Negotiated Sale of Such Note to Capital Bank, a Division of First Tennessee Bank National Association, Pursuant to the Terms and Conditions of a Loan Agreement by and Among the Florida Municipal Loan Council, the Village of Estero, Florida, and Capital Bank, a Division of First Tennessee Bank National Association; Approving the Form of and Authorizing the Execution and Delivery of the Loan Agreement; Making Certain Other Covenants and Agreements in Connection with the Issuance of Such Note; and Providing an Effective Date The title of Resolution No. 2019-01 was read by Villager Clerk Hall. Mayor Boesch noted that no changes have been made to the loan that council talked previously about. Village Manager Sarkozy provided explanation of the resolution; fixed rate and no pre- payment penalty. The plan is to close on January 14. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Wilson had questions on the closing statement, we will be receiving rent; who owns the ministry building? Village Manager Sarkozy responded the Village will own it; leases will be renewed as well as discussion of no additional liability or exposure. Finance Director Roberson added that insurance carriers are working together at the current time. Councilmember Batos asked about the $2200 for ad valorem taxes on the closing statement. John Paulich responded that was for the 2 weeks, paying January 1 through January 14, 2019; Finance Director Roberson added further information regarding payment of taxes. Councilmember Levitan - asked about this year's taxes; Finance Director Roberson responded. Public Comment: None Motion: Move to approve Resolution No. 2019-01 Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Questions or Comments: None Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 4 of 8 Action: Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: (c) Lien Removal Request on Property Located at 9870 Horne Lane Village Attorney Saunders explained that this item is to remove a lien that was in error. Council approval is required to remove the lien. Community Development Director Gibbs added further information. The lien was recorded and now needs Council action to remove. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Batos asked whether the lien would be placed on the correct property. Community Development Director Gibbs responded that there is a new owner who is removing the items. Councilmember Levitan asked that staff come up with a policy regarding liens to make certain it is formalized. He asked if this could be delegated to the special magistrate? Village Attorney Saunders said it could certainly be done; they will work with staff to put something together. Public Comment: Lauren Willison, 9840 Horne Lane, Estero, stated a concern that the fire department is purchasing property that is zoned agriculture. Community Development Director Gibbs noted that they will need to go through the public hearing process to rezone the property; Mayor Boesch suggested Ms Willison reach out to the fire chief. Motion: Move to remove lien on Property Located at 9870 Horne Lane Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Questions or Comments: None Action: Move to approve removal of lien Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 5 of 8 8. SECOND READING / PUBLIC HEARING OF ORDINANCES: (a) Ordinance No. 2019-13-(Renumbered from 2018-20) An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Repealing Development District Provisions of the Lee County Code Previously Adopted by Reference; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing and Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance No. 2019-13. Mayor Boesch asked whether the ordinance had been properly advertised; Village Clerk Hall responded affirmatively. Village Attorney Saunders provided an explanation. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2019-13. Motion by: Vice Mayor Ribble Seconded by: Councilmember Errington Questions or Comments: Action: Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: (b) = Ordinance No. 2019-01 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Adopting a Reserve Policy; and Providing an Effective Date Mayor Boesch opened the public hearing and read the title of Ordinance No. 2019-01. Village Manager Sarkozy provided background regarding incorporation; spoke to the reserves being set up as a "cushion", along with litigation reserve. A future Council would not be limited to changes, but they would have to do an ordinance revision. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Batos asked if we have a twelve-month reserve and if inflation increases, we would raise the amount the following year? Finance Director Roberson responded regarding adjustments each year. Councilmember Batos asked about "at twelve months or greater." Finance Director Roberson responded. Councilmember Levitan spoke to a super majority process. Councilmember Batos asked if twelve months is equal to other municipalities? Finance Director Roberson believes that other municipalities are between four and twelve months. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to adopt Ordinance No. 2019-01. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 6 of 8 10. 11. 12. Questions or Comments: None Action: Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Vice Mayor Ribble congratulated Councilmember Wilson on his recent presentation at ECCL and asked Village Attorney Saunders about regulation of vacation rentals. Village Attorney Saunders will research and get back to Council. Councilmember McLain asked about fill trucks speeding on Estero Parkway. After they dump, they are creating an issue with turning left and asked that Village Manager Sarkozy reach out to the Sheriff. Councilmember Errington stated that this is an historic day for the village in purchasing the Estero on the River property and thanked the D'Jamoos family. Councilmembers discussed appropriate naming of something for Don Eslick. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: Village Attorney Saunders spoke to the vision of the Council and how they exercised that vision. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: Village Manager Sarkozy conveyed appreciation to the staff who have helped on the purchase of the property. Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop 13. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Discussion: 20/20 Program for Conserving 10 Acres along the Estero River, Village of Estero Conservation 20/20 Nomination 571 Village Manager Sarkozy stated this property has been before Council previously and there's been an effort to secure 20/20 funding for this parcel from Lee County; led by the Conservation Foundation of the Gulf Coast. Debi Osborne, Director of Land Protection, is here to support the effort. : Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 9, 2019 Page 7 of 8 Conservation Foundation of the Gulf Coast, Debi Osborne, pledged $15,000 and would be willing to continue fund raising. Village Manager Sarkozy spoke regarding maintenance and the cost of invasive exotics removal, which could be $30,000. Questions or Comments: The Council discussed the cost of removing exotics. After discussion, they suggested that Ms. Osborne go forward before the commissioners. Public Comment: None. (b) Update: Parks, Recreation and Open Space Master Plan Village Manager Sarkozy stated the there will be a survey mailed to a number of people and focus group meetings with Council members. There will also be opportunities for general public comment. Community Development Director Gibbs spoke regarding the upcoming public meeting on Monday, January 14 at the Estero Recreation Center. Questions or Comments: None Public Comment: None A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 14. ADJOURNMENT: 11:14am. CALL TO ORDER: 9:30 a.m. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Mayor Boesch. INVOCATION: Father Tony Gilborges from Our Lady of Light Catholic Community. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Assistant Village Attorney Derek Rooney, Land Use Attorney Nancy Stroud, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. 2. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Batos Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Action: Approved the agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: 3. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - January 30, 2019 Page 1 of 3 4, WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Village of Estero "Recognition Policy" for Special Contributions to Community Village Manager Sarkozy provided a brief explanation of the purpose for the workshop. "Recognition Policy" for Special Contribution to Community that is easily administered and it can be uniform to apply in the future. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers commented how to honor Mr. Eslick. There were various suggestions including renaming Estero Parkway, or part of a park in his honor. (b) Review of Design Review Board Process Village Manager Sarkozy spoke regarding vacancies and part of the issue is lack of qualified landscape architects, among other reasons. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan commented how the board was designed on the Boca Raton concept. Councilmembers commented on how to move forward with the review board. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Deputy Village Clerk Tammy Duran. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS Motion: Move to approve the agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Vice Mayor Ribble Action: Approved the agenda. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) Financial Report for the Month Ended September 30, 2018 (b) Financial Report for the Month Ended January 31, 2019 (c) October 24, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes October 31, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes November 7, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes November 28, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes January 9, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes Public Comment: None Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: | Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous Nay: Abstentions: Questions or Comments: None 5. CONSIDERATION OF ITEMS DEFERRED FROM CONSENT AGENDA: None 6. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Discussion of Patty Whitehead as Member on Design Review Board Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Village Attorney Saunders suggested to Councilmember Errington, she not be part of the discussions to avoid any perception of a conflict of interest. Councilmember Errington agreed and recused herself from the discussion and the vote. Village Attorney Saunders discussed the rules and procedures of council and advisory boards. He stated there are three options the Council can act on today: let Ms. Whitehead's term expire at the end of March, remove Ms. Whitehead from the board, or reappoint Ms. Whitehead when her term expires. Questions or Comments: Mayor Boesch made the recommendation to end Ms. Whitehead's term on the board for the sake of establishing our highest credibility with the Village. Councilor Levitan said he would defend Ms. Whitehead's right to post her personal opinions about the government on Facebook, but he said her behavior discredited the Village. Councilor Batos said there are ramifications for what people post on social media. Public Comment: None Village Council Meeting Agenda - March 6, 2019 Page 2 of 6 10. Motion: Move to approve removing Patty Whitehead from the Design Review Board effective immediately. Motion by: Vice Mayor Ribble Seconded by: Mayor Boesch Vote: Aye: Councilmembers Levitan, McLain, Batos, Wilson, Vice Mayor Ribble, Mayor Boesch Nay: Abstentions: Councilmember Errington Questions or Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy stated staff will immediately post the position. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS Councilmember Batos informed the Council that the Estero Education Initiative distributed its first funds to the schools. Each school received six thousand dollars. Councilmember Levitan suggested the Council send back the zoning case (Estero Crossing) that the Planning and Zoning Board were unable to make a decision on. He suggested the Planning and Zoning Board reschedule the vote when all seven members are there. Village Attorney suggested it be put on the next agenda and have a vote on whether Council should send it back to the Planning and Zoning Board. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT Village Attorney Saunders stated that in reference to items discussed at the last meeting, he will provide sample ordinances on naming facilities. There is also a bill pending in the State that will prohibit local governments from regulating scooters on sidewalks. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop Items: 10:02am 11. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Mine Studies Peer Review Village Manager Sarkozy gave an introduction. Consultant of Spikowski presented a PowerPoint presentation with a description on how mining activity occurs, mining related to the amount of growth, and compared the methodology of the Waldrop and Stuart Reports. Village Council Meeting Agenda - March 6, 2019 Page 3 of 6 Questions or Comments: Councilmembers had a discussion on how to use this report, what is the best course of action the Village should take, putting a coalition together, and legal review of options. Public Comment: Susan Prock, Andore Lane, stated that knowledge is power and the Council should inform the public on their findings regarding how mining can affect our water supply. Bill Carr, Carriage Court, is encouraged with Council's comments and their desire to preserve our clean water. Kathy Dobash, Bacon Ave, is grateful to Council for acting on this issue and encourages them to keep fighting. Robert King, ECCL, believes the proposed amendment on mining should not go through. Joseph Stigliano, Tripoli Court, stated there are effects to their homes when mining occurs. Greg Stuart, Stuart & Associates, representing Sakata Seed Corp., stated the county has enough lime rock for forty-two years and Lee County just pulled the public hearings regarding the mining. Joel Avila, Ashcomb Way, stated Lee County should not dismiss this report. Lou Frattarelli, Black Tree Lane, is against any changes to the Mining Comprehensive Plan. Don Eslick, ECCL, feels any city that is downstream of the mining would be most impacted by it. Peter Cangialosi, San Georgio Drive, feels Lee County is not communicating with the public. The Council discussion included sending a letter to the Lee County Commissioners and contacting Commissioners individually. The Council requested the Village's Land Use Attorney provide a legal memorandum explaining options. Consensus from Council. (b) Design Review Board and Planning and Zoning Board Ordinance Amendment Village Manager Sarkozy gave an introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs explained the amendment. The proposed changes are to allow Council and/or the Village Manager thirty days to file an appeal of Design Review Board decisions. This ordinance will also apply to the Planning and Zoning Board. Staff will provide a staff report to the Design Review Board to assist the board with their analysis. The ordinance will first go to Planning and Zoning Board for a recommendations and then it will be scheduled for the first and second readings. Village Council Meeting Agenda - March 6, 2019 Page 4 of 6 Questions or Comments: Councilmember Batos asked if Community Development Director Gibbs can raise an appeal on her own. Village Attorney Saunders said she can through the Village Manger and explained Council can also raise an appeal. Public Comment: None There was consensus from Council to move forward. (c) Stormwater Master Plan Update Village Manager Sarkozy gave an introduction. Elizabeth Fountain and Zac Taylor, J.R. Evans Engineering, presented a PowerPoint presentation regarding the problem areas, observations, most important areas that flood, explained the regional model, proposed nine projects that are important to address, and one maintenance project. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers discussed how to get partnerships with communities that own some of the land that has been labeled as problem areas. Public Comment: None The meeting went into recess at 12:13 p.m. and reconvened at 12:20 p.m. Don Duke, FGCU Department of Marine and Ecological Sciences, presented a PowerPoint presentation regarding FGCU "Communities in Transition" initiative Stormwater and Drainage Research. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers suggested Professor Duke return with his students so Council can congratulate them on their work in person and also suggested that FGCU put together a log of all the different research projects that the University is doing so if Council or anyone else is looking for a particular research, they know who to reach out to. 12. It applies to members of advisory and non-advisory bodies who are presented with a voting conflict of interest under Section 112.3143, Florida Statutes. Your responsibilities under the law when faced with voting on a measure in which you have a conflict of interest will vary greatly depending on whether you hold an elective or appointive position. For this reason, please pay close attention to the instructions on this form before completing and filing the form. INSTRUCTIONS FOR COMPLIANCE WITH SECTION 112.3143, FLORIDA STATUTES A person holding elective or appointive county, municipal, or other local public office MUST ABSTAIN from voting on a measure which would inure to his or her special private gain or loss. Each elected or appointed local officer also MUST ABSTAIN from knowingly voting on a measure which would inure to the special gain or loss of a principal (other than a government agency) by whom he or she is retained (including the parent, subsidiary, or sibling organization of a principal by which he or she is retained); to the special private gain or loss of a relative; or to the special private gain or loss of a business associate. Commissioners of community redevelopment agencies (CRAs) under Sec. 163.356 or 163.357, F.S., and officers of independent special tax districts elected on a one-acre, one-vote basis are not prohibited from voting in that capacity. For purposes of this law, a "relative" includes only the officer's father, mother, son, daughter, husband, wife, brother, sister, father-in-law, mother-in-law, son-in-law, and daughter-in-law. A "business associate" means any person or entity engaged in or carrying on a business enterprise with the officer as a partner, joint venturer, coowner of property, or corporate shareholder (where the shares of the corporation are not listed on any national or regional stock exchange). * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * ELECTED OFFICERS: In addition to abstaining from voting in the situations described above, you must disclose the conflict: PRIOR TO THE VOTE BEING TAKEN by publicly stating to the assembly the nature of your interest in the measure on which you are abstaining from voting; and WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER THE VOTE OCCURS by completing and filing this form with the person responsible for recording the minutes of the meeting, who should incorporate the form in the minutes. * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * APPOINTED OFFICERS: Although you must abstain from voting in the situations described above, you are not prohibited by Section 112.3143 from otherwise participating in these matters. However, you must disclose the nature of the conflict before making any attempt to influence the decision, whether orally or in writing and whether made by you or at your direction. IF YOU INTEND TO MAKE ANY ATTEMPT TO INFLUENCE THE DECISION PRIOR TO THE MEETING AT WHICH THE VOTE WILL BE TAKEN: * You must complete and file this form (before making any attempt to influence the decision) with the person responsible for recording the minutes of the meeting, who will incorporate the form in the minutes. title: Village Council Meeting Minutes - 2019-03-20
raw_text: This Final Action Agenda/Minutes is supplemented by electronic recordings of the meeting, which may be reviewed upon request to the Village Clerk. Village Council meetings from June 8, 2016 forward can be viewed online at http://estero-fl.gov/council/watch-meetings-online/. Staff reports, resolutions, ordinances, and other documents related to this meeting are available at https://estero- fl.gov/agendas/ at the corresponding agenda date. APPROVED BY COUNCIL APRIL 3, 2019 FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES VILLAGE COUNCIL MEETING Village of Estero Council Chambers 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 March 20, 2019 = 9:30 a.m. 1. OATH OF OFFICE: Honorable Elisabeth Adams, 20" Judicial Circuit Judge Jon M. McLain - District 3 Katy Errington - District 4 James R. Boesch - District 5 James V. Wilson - District 7 2. CALL TO ORDER AND PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Mayor Boesch 3. INVOCATION: Pastor Nolen Rollins from Legacy Church 4. ROLL CALL Present: Mayor Jim Boesch - District 5, Vice Mayor Bill Ribble - District 1, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Village Land Use Counsel Nancy Stroud, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Deputy Village Clerk Tammy Duran 5. PROCLAMATIONS: (a) Tish Sargent - American Heart Association Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 1 of 7 ELECTION OF MAYOR Councilmember Levitan nominated Councilmember Bill Ribble for Mayor. There were no further nominations. Motion: Move to elect Councilmember Ribble as Mayor. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Elected Councilmember Ribble as Mayor. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: ELECTION OF VICE MAYOR Councilmember Wilson nominated Councilmember Errington as Vice Mayor. There were no other nominations. Motion: Move to elect Councilmember Errington as Vice Mayor. Motion by: Councilmember Wilson Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Elected Councilmember Errington as Vice Mayor. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: The meeting went into recess at 9:47 a.m. and reconvened at 9:53 a.m. APPROVAL OF AGENDA, ADDITIONS, AND DELETIONS: Councilmember Levitan asked that Agenda Item 12(a) Discuss Remand of Estero Crossing Zoning Case to Planning and Zoning Board be removed. After further review of the Zoning and Planning Meeting he discovered there was a second vote after their original vote. The original vote would stand. Motion: Move to approve the agenda as amended, pulling 12 (a). Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Action: Approved the agenda as amended, pulling 12 (a). Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 2 of 7 9. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: None. 10. CONSENT AGENDA: (a) December 5, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes December 12, 2018 Council Meeting Minutes January 23, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes January 30, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes February 6, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes February 20, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes March 6, 2019 Council Meeting Minutes Public Comment: None. Questions or Comments: None. Motion: Move to approve the Consent Agenda. Motion by: Councilmember McLain Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Approved the Consent Agenda. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 11. CONSIDERATION OF ITEMS DEFERRED FROM CONSENT AGENDA: None. 12. ACTION ITEMS: (a) Discuss Remand of Estero Crossing Zoning Case to Planning and Zoning Board Agenda Item 12 (a) was pulled from the agenda. (b) Planning and Zoning Board and Design Review Board Items Recommended Action: e Reappoint Planning and Zoning Board members Marlene Naratil and James Tatooles for a one-year term expiring March 31, 2020. e Reappoint Design Review Board member Barry Jones for a one-year term expiring on March 31, 2020. e Appoint a Chairman for each Board. e Authorize staff to advertise for the three (3) vacant positions for Design Review Board, including a landscape architect per the Ordinance requirement. e Designate which Design Review Board member is considered the State-registered architect for purposes of determining a quorum. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 3 of 7 Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs explained why this was being brought up to Council at this time. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan suggested the Council take each recommendation separately. Vice Mayor Errington expressed concern about not having a landscape architect. Community Development Director Gibbs explained that the Village will reach out to firms for assistance if needed. Public Comment: None. Motion: Move to reappoint Planning and Zoning Board Members Marlene Naratil and James Tatooles for a full three-year term expiring March 31, 2022. Reappoint Chairman Scotty Wood to the Planning and Zoning Board. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Batos Action: Reappointed Planning and Zoning Board Members Marlene Naratil and James Tatooles for a full three-year term expiring March 31, 2022. Reappointed Chairman Scotty Wood to the Planning and Zoning Board. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Motion: Move to reappoint Design Review Board Member Barry Jones to a two-year term expiring on March 31, 2021. Appoint Barry Jones as Chair of the Design Review Board. Bill Glass will be the Design Review Board Member considered the State-registered architect for purpose of determining a quorum. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Boesch Action: Reappointed Design Review Board Member Barry Jones to a two-year term expiring on March 31, 2021. Appointed Barry Jones as Chair of the Design Review Board. Bill Glass will be the Design Review Board Member considered the State-registered architect for purpose of determining a quorum. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: Motion: Authorize staff to advertise for the three (3) vacant positions for Design Review Board, including a landscape architect per the Ordinance requirement. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember Wilson Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 4 of 7 Action: Authorized staff to advertise for the three (3) vacant positions for Design Review Board, including a landscape architect per the Ordinance requirement. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 13. FIRST READING OF ORDINANCE Ordinance No 2019-22 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Further Amending Ordinance No. 15-01 Regarding Appeals From the Village Planning and Zoning Board and the Design Review Board; Amending Ordinance 15-01 to Require Written Recommendations From the Village Manager or His Designee to the Design Review Board; Providing a Severability Clause; and Providing an Effective Date Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs explained the ordinance. The Planning and Zoning Board reviewed the ordinance last night at their meeting and there was some confusion on the wording in the ordinance. The Planning and Zoning Board requested that the language be modified before the second reading. Questions or Comments: Councilmember Levitan explained to Council that Village Land Use Counsel Stroud will be working on modifying the language and will have it to Council well before the second reading on April 17". Councilmember Batos stated the suggestion of the Planning and Zoning Board that Council take jurisdiction of cases is one he agrees with. Motion: Move to pass first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-22. Motion by: Councilmember Levitan Seconded by: Councilmember McLain Action: Passed first reading of Ordinance No. 2019-22. Vote: Aye: Unanimous (Roll call vote) Nay: Abstentions: 14. PUBLIC COMMENT ON NON-AGENDA ITEMS: Robert King, representing ECCL, stated that there are several County Comprehensive Plan amendments in the DRGR area. Council should consider all eight amendments under the DRGR. They will all have an effect on the Village if they are passed. 15. COUNCIL COMMUNICATIONS / FUTURE AGENDA ITEMS: Councilmember Levitan stated the Village needs to move quickly on the mining issues with Lee County. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 5 of 7 16. 17. Vice Mayor Errington stated she wrote a letter to the Lee County Commissioners regarding the mining. Councilmember Boesch stated he sent a letter to the five mayors of the cities seeking their support in opposition to Lee County's repeal of the restrictions on mining in the DRGR. He also was a signer on a letter with the five other mayors that was sent to Governor DeSantis regarding the Caloosahatchee minimum flow and level rule. Councilmember Wilson had concerns with how the Village could remind voters to reregister for the vote by mail ballot. He discovered that they expire and right now Lee County does not send out reminders to voters to reregister for the vote by mail ballot. VILLAGE ATTORNEY'S REPORT: None. VILLAGE MANAGER'S REPORT: None. The meeting went into recess at 10:48 a.m. and reconvened at 11:00 a.m. Adjourn Regular Session and Convene Workshop 18. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Estero Branding Update Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Public Works Director Willems presented a PowerPoint presentation on the results of the data collected from the surveys and focus groups the Village had conducted. On the April 3"! Village Council meeting, the consultants will have a report on their interpretation of the data. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers had questions and comments regarding the signage in the Village and when staff thought the signs would be up. Public Comment: None. (b) Code Enforcement Mitigation Village Manager Sarkozy provided an introduction. Community Development Director Gibbs explained how the Village code enforcement process works. Questions or Comments: Councilmembers had questions and comments regarding the process of collecting fines from code violations, lien process, and if the Village should hire someone totry and collect the fines. Village Attorney Saunders stated he would work with staff and come up with a strategy on how the Village could collect. Village Manager Sarkozy stated staff would come back at the next Council meeting with a strategy on how to collect, imposing fines, demand for payment, and legal action the Village could take. Public Comment: None. Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 6 of 7 A motion to adjourn was made and duly passed. 12. ADJOURNMENT: 11:48p.m. ATTEST: ; VILLAGE OF ESTERO, FLORIDA By: Si LAWL By: thi Mel, Kat hy Hall, MMC, Village Clerk Bill Ribble, Mayor Village Council Meeting Final Action Agenda/Minutes - March 20, 2019 Page 7 of 7
